theorem.class10.equal_chords_arcs.step1_.text: 🔗 Doosre circle par do barabar chords AB aur CD draw karein, jaise do barabar lambai ke strings ek circular instrument par.

theorem.class10.equal_chords_arcs.step2_.text: OA = OB = OC = OD = r aur AB = CD. Isliye triangles SSS ke adhar par barabar hain.

theorem.class10.equal_chords_arcs.step2_: Kendra ko endpoints tak jod do.

theorem.class10.equal_chords_arcs.step3_.text: Triangleon ki barabarata se, central angles barabar hain. Barabar central angles barabar arcs ka matlab hain.

theorem.class10.equal_chords_arcs.title: Theorem 9.7: Barabar Chords → Saman Arcs
Barabar chords centre se ek hi distance par hain.
Do guitar strings (chords) ek hi length ke hain, jo circular sound hole ke centre se ek hi distance par hain. Iska upyog circular structures mein balon ko balance karne ke liye kiya jata hai.
Equal chord centre se ek hi door hain.
Equal chord centre se ek hi door hain.
Equal chord → centre se equal distance.
Centre se 6 unit door do equal chord 16 ki lambai ke hain. Kya distance hai?
✏️ Dua chord AB aur CD ek hi lambai ke hain. Centre se har chord par perpendicular OE aur OF khinchayein.
Theorem 9.3 ke anusaar, E aur F midpoints hain. AE = AB/2, CF = CD/2. Kyunki AB = CD, AE = CF.
Perpendicular bisector property ka upyog karein
Right triangles OAE aur OCF mein, kyunki OA = OC (radius) aur AE = CF: OE² = OF² → OE = OF.
Theorem 9.4: Equal Chords are Equidistant from Centre
theorem.class10.equal_tangents.finalForm: Ek circle ke external point se do tangents ki lambai barabar hoti hai.
theorem.class10.equal_tangents.keyInsigh: Aap ek point se circle par do tangents draw karte hain, toh unki lambai har baar hi barabar hoti hai. Iska istemaal V-belt drives ko design karne mein hota hai, jahan pulleys ke dono side equal hoti hain!
theorem.class10.equal_tangents.practice_: Ek hi point se tangents samaan hote hain
Dono tangents A se ek hi hain: AB = AC = 12 cm.
Ek hi bāhir ke point se do tangents hote hain, unka bharosa samaan hai.
Point A se ek circle par do tangents khinchte hain. Agar ek tangent 12 cm ka hai, to doosra kitna lamba hai?
theorem.class10.equal_tangents.step1_det: 📍 Point A ko circle ke bahar le jayein. Ab circle par do tangents AB aur AC draw karein, jo circle par B aur C points par touch karte hain.
theorem.class10.equal_tangents.step1_lab: External point se tangents draw karein
theorem.class10.equal_tangents.step2_det: OA, OB, OC ko jodein. Theorem 10.2 ke anusaar: OB AB ke saath khatra hai aur OC AC ke saath khatra hai, isliye ∠OBA = ∠OCA = 90°.
theorem.class10.equal_tangents.step3_det: OB = OC (radii), OA = OA (samaan), ∠OBA = ∠OCA = 90°. RHS samanata → AB = AC.
theorem.class10.equal_tangents.title: Theorem 10.3: External Point se do Tangents Barabar hain
theorem.class10.equidistant_chords.final: Centre se ek hi door ke chord ek hi lambai ke hain.
theorem.class10.equidistant_chords.keyIn: This completes the "if and only if": chords are equal ↔ equidistant. This symmetry is fundamental in circular grid design and radial structures like Ferris wheels.
theorem.class10.equidistant_chords.pract: Bara bar centre se samaan doori
Yes, chords at equal distance from the centre are always equal in length.
Equal distances from centre → equal chords.
Two chords are both 8 units from the centre of a circle of radius 10. Are they equal?
theorem.class10.equidistant_chords.step1: 🎪 Draw two chords AB and CD such that their perpendicular distances from the centre (OE and OF) are equal.
theorem.class10.equidistant_chords.step2: Since OA = OC (radii) and OE = OF: AE² = CF² → AE = CF.
theorem.class10.equidistant_chords.step3: E and F are midpoints (Theorem 9.3), so AB = 2×AE and CD = 2×CF. Since AE = CF, we get AB = CD.
theorem.class10.equidistant_chords.title: Theorem 9.5: Centre se ek hi door ke chord ek hi lambai ke hain.

theorem.class10.ex_radius.finalFormulaDe.key: The radius of the excircle opposite vertex A.
theorem.class10.ex_radius.finalFormulaDe.value: Vertex A ke khilaf ex-circle ki radius

theorem.class10.ex_radius.keyInsight.key: Exradii are always larger than the inradius. Each ex-circle touches one side of the triangle and the extensions of the other two sides. Used in advanced geometry and triangle geometry problems.
theorem.class10.ex_radius.keyInsight.value: Exradii hamesha inradius se bade hote hain. Har ex-circle triangle ki ek side aur doosri do sides ki extensions ko chhuta hai. Advanced geometry aur triangle geometry ke problems mein istemal hota hai.

theorem.class10.ex_radius.practice_expla.text: r₁ ≈ 3.68. Compare with inradius r ≈ 2.10 — exradius is larger!

theorem.class10.ex_radius.step1_detail.key: 🔵 Draw the ex-centre I₁ opposite vertex A. Connect it to all three vertices, creating three regions.
theorem.class10.ex_radius.step1_detail.value: 🔵 A ke khilaf ex-centre I₁ ko draw karein. Isse sabhi teen vertices se judaayein, teen regions banayein.

theorem.class10.ex_radius.step1_label.value: A ke khilaf ex-circle ko draw karein

theorem.class10.ex_radius.step2_detail.key: Δ = Area(I₁AB) + Area(I₁AC) − Area(I₁BC) because I₁BC is outside the original triangle.
theorem.class10.ex_radius.step2_detail.value: Δ = Area(I₁AB) + Area(I₁AC) − Area(I₁BC) kyunki I₁BC original triangle ke bahar hai.

theorem.class10.ex_radius.step3_detail.key: b + c − a = 2S − 2a = 2(S−a). So Δ = ½r₁ × 2(S−a) = r₁(S−a).
theorem.class10.ex_radius.step3_detail.value: b + c − a = 2S − 2a = 2(S−a). Isliye Δ = ½r₁ × 2(S−a) = r₁(S−a).

theorem.class10.external_touching.finalF: Agar circles bāhir se touch karte hain, to centres ke beech ki doori radii ke sum ke barabar hoti hai.
theorem.class10.external_touching.keyIns: Do soap bubbles milne par, unke centres ko unke radii ke sum se alag karta hai. Isi liye ball bearings inner aur outer races ke beech perfect seat karte hain.

theorem.class10.external_touching.practi.text: Do circle jo radius 3 cm aur 4 cm ke hain, external touch karte hain. Centre ke beech ki distance kya hai?

theorem.class10.external_touching.step1_.text: 🔵🔵 A aur B ke kendra par do circle draw karein jo ek hi point C par touch karte hain.

theorem.class10.external_touching.step1_: Do circles bāhir se touch karte hain

theorem.class10.external_touching.step2_.text: A aur B ke beech ki sabse kam distance point of contact C se guzarti hai.
Point C yeh line AB par hai

theorem.class10.external_touching.step3_.text: AC radius r₁ hai, BC radius r₂ hai. Kyunki C line AB par hai: AB = r₁ + r₂.

theorem.class10.external_touching.title: Theorem 10.4: Bāhir se Touching Circles
theorem.class10.half_angle_cos.finalForm: Cosines ke half-angle ko side lengths ke roop mein direkt vyakt karein.
theorem.class10.half_angle_cos.keyInsigh: Ye formulas aapko half-angles ke trigonometric functions ko calculate karne ke liye calculator ki zaroorat nahin hai - sirf side lengths ki zaroorat hai! Ye lunar aur planetary calculations mein upyog kiye jaate hain.

theorem.class10.half_angle_cos.practice_.text: a=5, b=6, c=7. S=9. α/2 ke liye cos ko khojain.

theorem.class10.half_angle_cos.step1_det.text: 📊 α ke liye cos = (b²+c²−a²)/(2bc). Yeh cos ke full angle ko side lengths ke hisaab se deta hai.

theorem.class10.half_angle_cos.step1_lab: Cosines ke Law se shuru karein.

theorem.class10.half_angle_cos.step3_det.text: Semi-perimeter S = (a+b+c)/2 ka istemal karke algebra, hum paate hain: cos²(α/2) = S(S−a)/(bc).

theorem.class10.half_angle_cos.step3_lab.text: α/2 ke liye cos² ko hal karin

theorem.class10.half_angle_cos.step4_det.text: Positive square root lekar half-angle cosine milta hai. Yeh formula side lengths ke hisaab se trigonometric ratio deta hai!

theorem.class10.half_angle_cos.title: Half Angle Formula for Cosines
theorem.class10.half_angle_sin.finalForm: Half Angle Formula for Sines ko side lengths ke roop mein direkt vyakt karein.
theorem.class10.half_angle_sin.keyInsigh: Cosine half-angle formula ke saath, ye Hero's formula aur navigation aur astronomy mein aakarshak gyaan ke liye upyog kiye jaate hain!

theorem.class10.half_angle_sin.practice_.text: a=5, b=6, c=7. S=9. sin(α/2) ko khojein.

theorem.class10.half_angle_sin.step1_det: Cos α = 1 − 2sin²(α/2) se shuru karein, aur phir Law of Cosines ka upyog karein.
theorem.class10.half_angle_sin.step2_det: Algebraic manipulation ke baad, hamen milta hai ki sin²(α/2) = (S−b)(S−c)/(bc).
theorem.class10.half_angle_sin.step3_det: Cosine half-angle formula ke saath, ye navigation aur astronomy mein aakarshak gyaan ke liye upyog kiye jaate hain!
theorem.class10.half_angle_sin.title: Half Angle Formula for Sines
theorem.class10.heros_formula.finalFormu: Ek triangle ke teen side kee lon kee madad se uska kshetrafal nikalna.
theorem.class10.heros_formula.keyInsight: Yeh 2,000 varsh pahle ka formula Hero of Alexandria ne banaya tha, jo sirf side kee lon par nirbhar karta hai — koi angle, koi trigonometry nahin! Aaj bhi yeh formula land surveying, GPS kshetrafal ka gunjana, aur computer graphics mein upyog hota hai.

theorem.class10.heros_formula.practice_e.text: Δ = √900 = 30. Yeh ek 5-12-13 right triangle hai!

theorem.class10.heros_formula.practice_q: a=5, b=12, c=13. S aur kshetrafal ko khojna.
theorem.class10.heros_formula.step1_deta: 📏 Aapne ek triangular bagicha ke teen side kee lon: a=5m, b=6m, c=7m, maap liye hain. Shuru karein Δ = ½ bc sin α se.
theorem.class10.heros_formula.step1_labe: Shuru karein Δ = ½ bc sin α se.
theorem.class10.heros_formula.step2_deta: Double-angle identity ka upyog karke: sin α = 2 sin(α/2) cos(α/2). Ab half-angle formulas ko daal dein.
theorem.class10.heros_formula.step2_labe: Sin α = 2 sin(α/2) cos(α/2) ka upyog karein.
theorem.class10.heros_formula.step3_deta: Sin(α/2) = √[(S−b)(S−c)/(bc)] aur cos(α/2) = √[S(S−a)/(bc)] ko daal kar, bc kee gatividhiin bat jati hain, aur Hero ka formula aata hai!
theorem.class10.heros_formula.title: Hero ka Formula (SSS)

theorem.class10.in_radius.finalFormulaDe.text: Ek triangle ke andar ek circle ki radius. Iska radius ek triangle ke andar ek circle ki radius hai.

theorem.class10.in_radius.keyInsight.text: Incircle woh sabse bada circle hai jo ek triangle ke andar fit hota hai. Yeh formula use hota hai ek triangular box ke andar ek pizza ki radius nikalne ke liye — inradius!

theorem.class10.in_radius.practice_expla.text: r = 2. Ek 6-8-10 right triangle ka incircle radius 2 hota hai.

theorem.class10.in_radius.practice_quest.text: a=6, b=8, c=10. Area=24. Inradius nikalein.

theorem.class10.in_radius.step1_detail.text: Incircle sabhi teen hisson ko triangle ke andar se chhua jata hai. Iska centre I angle bisectors ke intersection par hota hai.

theorem.class10.in_radius.step2_detail.text: Incentre I triangle ΔABC ko teen chhote triangle mein divide karta hai, har ek mein height r hota hai.

theorem.class10.in_radius.step2_label.text: Triangle ko teen hisson mein split karein

theorem.class10.in_radius.step3_detail.text: Δ = r(a+b+c)/2 = rS. Isliye r = Δ/S.

theorem.class10.internal_touching.finalF.text: Agar circle internal touch karte hain, to distance radii ki difference ke barabar hoti hai.

theorem.class10.internal_touching.keyIns.text: Ball bearings is principle ka istemaal karte hain: inner race (chhota circle) outer race (bada circle) internal touch karta hai ek hi point par.

theorem.class10.internal_touching.practi.text: Do circle radii 5 cm aur 2 cm ke touch internal karte hain. Centre ke beech ki distance?

theorem.class10.internal_touching.step1_.text: ⭕🔵 Ek bada circle (radius r₁) aur ek chhota circle (radius r₂) draw karein, jo C par touch karte hain.
Internal touch wale circle draw karein.

theorem.class10.internal_touching.step2_.text: Circle C par touch karte hain, jo line AB par hai. Chhota circle bada circle ke andar hai.

theorem.class10.internal_touching.step3_.text: Outer circle ke liye: AC = r₁. Inner circle ke liye: BC = r₂. Kyunki B andar hai: AB = r₁ − r₂.

theorem.class10.law_cosines.finalFormula.text: Pythagoras ka niyam kisi bhi triangle ke liye general hai, na keval right-angled triangle ke liye.

theorem.class10.law_cosines.keyInsight.text: Surveyors iska upyog karte hain land plots ki measurement karne ke liye. Agar aapko do sides aur unke beech angle (SAS) ka pata hai, to aap third side ko calculate kar sakte hain bina uski jaanakar!

theorem.class10.law_cosines.practice_que: b=5, c=6, α=60°. Side a ko khojein
theorem.class10.law_cosines.step1_detail: A se side BC par ek perpendicular AD girayein, do right triangles banayein

theorem.class10.law_cosines.step1_label.text: Altitude AD ko BC ke perpendicular banayein

theorem.class10.law_cosines.step2_detail: CD = a − c cos β. Altitude base ko do segments mein tod deti hai
theorem.class10.law_cosines.step3_detail: b² = c² sin²β + a² + c² cos²β − 2ac cos β
theorem.class10.law_cosines.step3_label: Pythagoras ko ADC ki right triangle mein lagayein
theorem.class10.law_cosines.step4_detail: b² = a² + c²(sin²β + cos²β) − 2ac cos β = a² + c² − 2ac cos β. Jab β = 90° hota hai, toh yeh Pythagorean Theorem hota hai!
theorem.class10.law_cosines.step4_label: sin²+cos²=1 ka upyog karke simplify karein
theorem.class10.law_sines.finalFormulaDe: Ek side ki ratio, uski opposite angle ki sine se, constant hoti hai.
theorem.class10.law_sines.keyInsight: GPS satellites iska istimal karte hain! Angles ko multiple satellites tak pahunchne se, aapka phone Sines ki kanoon ka istimal karke apni exact position ko kisi bhi jagah par triangulate karta hai.
theorem.class10.law_sines.practice_quest: a=7, α=50°, β=70°. Side b ko khojein.
theorem.class10.law_sines.step1_detail: 🌄 B se AC tak perpendicular kaakar likhein. Altitude h do tarahon se vyakt kiya ja sakta hai: h = c sin α aur h = a sin γ.
theorem.class10.law_sines.step1_label: B se AC tak altitude h kaakar likhein
theorem.class10.law_sines.step2_detail: Dono ek hi altitude ke barabar hain: c sin α = a sin γ → a/sin α = c/sin γ.
theorem.class10.law_sines.step3_detail: Dusre altitudes ke saath karke complete Sines ki kanoon prapt karein.
theorem.class10.law_sines.step3_label: Dusre sides ke liye bhi karein
theorem.class10.law_sines.title: Sines ki kanoon

theorem.class10.law_tangents.finalFormul.text: Side lengthon ko tangents of half angle sums aur differences se judata hai.

theorem.class10.law_tangents.keyInsight.text: Navigation aur surveying mein, Law of Tangents ko Law of Cosines se prefer kiya jata hai jab do sides aur shamil angle jaan kar, kyonki yeh square roots ko avoid karta hai aur cleaner results dete hain!

theorem.class10.law_tangents.step1_detai.text: 📐 a/sin α = b/sin β se, hum a/b = sin α/sin β milte hain.

theorem.class10.law_tangents.step1_label.text: Law of Sines se shuru karein

theorem.class10.law_tangents.step2_detai: Componendo-dividendo ka istemal karke: (a+b)/(a−b) = (sin α + sin β)/(sin α − sin β).
theorem.class10.law_tangents.step3_detai: sin α + sin β = 2 sin[(α+β)/2] cos[(α−β)/2] aur sin α − sin β = 2 cos[(α+β)/2] sin[(α−β)/2]. Is nishpaad ki upyogata hai tangent roop mein badalne ke liye.

theorem.class10.major_segment.finalFormu.text: Semicircle se adhik segment mein angle acute hota hai.

theorem.class10.major_segment.keyInsight.text: Theorems 10.11 ke saath milakar, circular segments mein angles ki puri shreni ki vistarit hoti hai. Semicircle 90° par ek boundary hai, acute aur obtuse ke beech.

theorem.class10.major_segment.practice_e.text: Major segment mein angles tikhon (< 90°) hote hain.

theorem.class10.major_segment.practice_q.text: Major segment mein ek angle: tikhon ya bhari hoti hai?

theorem.class10.major_segment.step1_deta.text: 📐 AB chord ka ek bada segment banayein (semicircle se bada). Ek point P major arc par chunein.

theorem.class10.major_segment.step1_labe.text: Chord AB se ek major segment banayein

theorem.class10.major_segment.step2_deta.text: Bada segment ka central angle 180° se kam hota hai.

theorem.class10.major_segment.step3_deta.text: Kyonki central angle 180° se kam hai, inscribed angle 90° se kam (tikhon) hai.

theorem.class10.minor_segment.finalFormu: Ek segment mein ek angle jo ek semicircle se kam hota hai, voh obtuse hota hai.
theorem.class10.minor_segment.keyInsight: Yeh classification complete karti hai: major segment → acute (< 90°), semicircle → right (= 90°), minor segment → obtuse (> 90°). Yeh sundar result right angles ko itna special kyun banata hai!

theorem.class10.minor_segment.practice_e.text: Ek minor segment mein angle 90° se zyada hota hai (obtuse).

theorem.class10.minor_segment.practice_q: Ek minor segment mein ek angle: acute ya obtuse hota hai?
theorem.class10.minor_segment.step1_deta: 📐 Ek chord AB draw karein jo ek minor segment (smaller than a semicircle) banata hai.
theorem.class10.minor_segment.step1_labe: Ek chord draw karein jo ek minor segment banata hai.
theorem.class10.minor_segment.step2_deta: Minor segment se subtended central angle 180° se bada hota hai.
theorem.class10.minor_segment.step3_deta: Ek angle jo 180° se bada hota hai, uska half ek inscribed angle jo 90° se bada hota hai.

theorem.class10.perp_bisects.finalFormul.text: Centre se perpendicular ek chord ko bisect karta hai.

theorem.class10.perp_bisects.keyInsight.text: Yeh Theorem 9.2 ka counter hai. In dono theorems ne chord ki calculations ki foundation banayi hai — arches, bridges, aur circular structures jaise Colosseum ka design karne mein istemal hoti hain!

theorem.class10.perp_bisects.practice_qu: Ek chord 16 units ka hai, jo centre se 6 units ka fasa hai. Toh radius kya hai?

theorem.class10.perp_bisects.step1_detai.text: 🎯 AB ek chord aur centre O se perpendicular draw karein jo AB par C par milta hai.

theorem.class10.perp_bisects.step2_detai.text: OA aur OB radii hain, isliye OA = OB. Triangle OAB isosceles hai.

theorem.class10.perp_bisects.step3_detai: OA = OB (radii), OC = OC (common), ∠OCA = ∠OCB = 90°. Isliye triangle congruent hain RHS ke liye. Isliye AC = BC.

theorem.class10.perp_bisects.title.text: Theorem 9.3: Centre se perpendicular ek chord ko bisects karta hai

theorem.class10.perp_radius_tangent.fina: A line perpendicular to a radius at its endpoint on the circle is a tangent.
theorem.class10.perp_radius_tangent.keyI: This is the construction method for tangents — simply draw a perpendicular at the endpoint of a radius. Used in designing roller coaster tracks and railway curves!

theorem.class10.perp_radius_tangent.prac.text: Radius ke endpoint par perpendicular line = tangent
Radius ke endpoint par perpendicular line kya hai?
Ek radius par endpoint par perpendicular line draw ki gayi hai. Iski kya hai?

theorem.class10.perp_radius_tangent.step.text: 🔄 Circle par ek radial segment OA centre O se point A tak draw karein. Circle par line PQ OA ⟂ se A par draw karein.
Kyonki OA perpendicular distance hai, isliye O se PQ tak ki sabse kam distance hai. PQ par koi bhi doosra point O se aur door hai.
O se PQ par koi bhi doosra point radius se bhi door hai, isliye koi bhi doosra point circle par nahin hai.
Ek line circle par ek hi point par touch karti hai, use tangent kaha jata hai. Ek radius ke endpoint par perpendicular draw karein aur tangent construct karein!

theorem.class10.quadratic_formula.finalF.text: Kisi bhi quadratic equation ko hal karne ke liye ek universal formula hai: ax² + bx + c = 0.

theorem.class10.quadratic_formula.keyIns.text: Discriminant D = b²−4ac aapko sab kuch batata hai: D > 0 ka matlab hai 2 real roots (parabola x-axis ko do baar cross karta hai), D = 0 ka matlab hai 1 root (ek baar touch karta hai), D < 0 ka matlab hai no real roots (kabhi bhi cross nahin karta). Architects iska use arches design karne ke liye karte hain jo perfect tarah se ek diya gaya distance span karte hain!

theorem.class10.quadratic_formula.practi.text: x = 2 ya 3. Quadratic formula dono roots find karta hai!
Formula ka upyog karke x² − 5x + 6 = 0 ka hal karein.

theorem.class10.quadratic_formula.step1_.text: Aap ek architect hain jo ek parabolic arch design kar rahe hain. Curve ax² + bx + c = 0 ke anusar chalti hai. Aapko koi bhi arch shape ke liye kaam karta formula chahiye. General quadratic equation se shuru karein.

theorem.class10.quadratic_formula.step2_.text: Cnstnt c ko dhai panh kar do: ax² + bx = −c. Arch ke vertical offset uski shape par asar nahi deta, sirf use upar ya neeche shift karta hai.

theorem.class10.quadratic_formula.step3_.text: A ko divide karein taaki x² ke coefficient 1 ke barabar ho jaye. Isse aage ki step: completing the square setup hoti hai.

theorem.class10.quadratic_formula.step4_.text: Dono panhon par (b/2a)² jodein: x² + (b/a)x + (b/2a)² = (b/2a)² − c/a. Bhai panh x² + (b/a)x + (b/2a)² ke left side simplify hota hai, aur right side (b²−4ac)/(4a²) ban jata hai.

theorem.class10.quadratic_formula.step5_.text: Square root lein: x + b/2a = ±√(b²−4ac)/(2a). X ko isolate karein aur Quadratic Formula prapt karein. Algebra mein is formula ka upyog sabse adhik kiya jata hai!

theorem.class10.same_segment.finalFormul.text: Sabhi angle, ek chord ko same segment mein subtend karne ke liye barabar hain.

theorem.class10.same_segment.keyInsight.text: Circular arc par koi bhi point, ek chord ko same angle se dekh sakta hai. Yeh circular sports stadiums mein 'goal line' ki principle hai — har seat ko same viewing angle milta hai!

theorem.class10.same_segment.practice_er: Ek segment mein aane wale angle barabar hote hain.
theorem.class10.same_segment.practice_ex: ∠ADB = 40° kyunki dono segment ke aane wale hain.
theorem.class10.same_segment.practice_hi: Ek segment mein aane wale angle barabar hote hain.

theorem.class10.same_segment.practice_qu.text: ∠ACB 40° segment mein hai. D bhi same segment par hai, to ∠ADB kitna hai?

theorem.class10.same_segment.step1_detai.text: 🎯 Chord AB ko draw karein. Same segment par circle ke points C, D, E lein.

theorem.class10.same_segment.step1_label.text: Chord AB ko lein aur same segment par points lein.

theorem.class10.same_segment.step2_detai.text: Ye sab angles, chord AB ko different points par same arc par subtend karte hain.

theorem.class10.same_segment.step3_detai.text: Ye sab, central angle ∠AOB (Theorem 10.7) ka half barabar hain. Kyunki ve same central angle ka half barabar hain, unhein barabar hona hi chahiye!

theorem.class10.same_segment.step3_label.text: Sabhi equal half of ∠AOB ke barabar hain.

theorem.class10.same_segment.title.text: Theorem 10.8: Angles in Same Segment are Equal

theorem.class10.semicircle.finalFormulaD: Ek semicircle mein inscribed angle har baar 90° hota hai.
theorem.class10.semicircle.keyInsight: Thales of Miletus (c. 600 BCE) ko attribute kiya jata hai, yeh ek se pehle jaana jana theorem hai. Egyptian pyramid builders ne iska use right angles banane ke liye kiya tha. Yeh GPS triangulation mein 'circle of possibilities' ka basis hai!

theorem.class10.semicircle.practice_ques.text: AB ek diameter hai. Point P semicircle par hai. ∠APB kya hai?

theorem.class10.semicircle.step1_detail: 🌓 AB ke diameter se ek circle banayein jo centre O se guzarta hai. Koi bhi point P ko semicircle arc par chun lein.

theorem.class10.semicircle.step2_detail.text: Chord AB ke liye central angle ∠AOB = 180° hai. Theorem 10.7 ke anusaar, ∠APB = ½ × 180° = 90°.

theorem.class10.semicircle.step2_label: Central angle theorem ko apply karein.

theorem.class10.semicircle.step3_detail.text: P semicircle arc par koi bhi jagah par ho, ∠APB 90° hi rahta hai!

theorem.class10.semicircle.step3_label: Koi bhi point P ke liye kaam karta hai.
theorem.class10.semicircle.title: Theorem 10.9: Ek Semicircle mein angle ek Right Angle hai.

theorem.class10.tangent_perp_radius.fina.text: Tangent aur point of contact par radius perpendicular hain.

theorem.class10.tangent_perp_radius.keyI.text: Yehi wajah hai ki ek bicycle wheel ke spokes (radii) ground (tangent) ke perpendicular hote hain contact point par. Yeh property wheel design aur railway engineering mein fundamental hai.

theorem.class10.tangent_perp_radius.prac: Tangent radius ke point of contact par perpendicular hai.

theorem.class10.tangent_perp_radius.prac.text: Radius OB tangent par B se milta hai. Unke beech kya angle hai?

theorem.class10.tangent_perp_radius.step.text: ⭕ Circle ka centre O par ek circle draw karein. Circle par PQ se tangent karte hue B par touch karein. Radius OB ko draw karein.
Tangent aur radius ko draw karein
Agar OB perpendicular nahin hai, to PQ par koi anya point hai jo O se B se kam hai.
Mante hain ki yeh dono alag-alag nahin hain
Yeh closer point circle ke andar hai, isliye PQ circle se do points par milta hai — yeh PQ ek tangent hai, iska matlab hai ki PQ circle se do points par milta hai.
Mante hain ki yeh dono alag-alag hain
Mante hain ki false hai, isliye OB PQ ke perpendicular hain. Radius aur tangent always perpendicular hote hain.
Isliye OB PQ ke perpendicular hain

theorem.class10.tangent_perp_radius.titl.text: Theorem 10.2: Tangent ⟂ Radius at Point of Contact

theorem.class10.three_point_circle.final.text: Three points not on a line uniquely define a circle.

theorem.class10.three_point_circle.keyIn.text: Yeh theorem yeh hai ki teen legs of ek tripod always touch the ground — koi bhi teen non-collinear points ek unique circle define karte hain. GPS trilateration isse principle use karta hai position locate karna!

theorem.class10.three_point_circle.pract.text: Kisi bhi teen non-collinear points ek circle define karte hain
Har triangle (3 non-collinear points) ek hi circumcircle define karta hai.
Har triangle ke paas unique circumcircle hota hai.
Teen points ek triangle banate hain jo sides 3, 4, 5 ke hain. Unke beech unique circle ko kya shape define karta hai?

theorem.class10.three_point_circle.step1.text: 📍 Aap kisi bhi teen points A, B, C chunein jo ek straight line par na ho. Unhein ek triangle banayein.

theorem.class10.three_point_circle.step2.text: Kisi bhi do sides ke perpendicular bisectors ko draw karein. Ve ek hi point O par milte hain, jo circumcentre hai.

theorem.class10.three_point_circle.step3.text: O AB ke bisector par hai, isliye OA = OB hai. Isi tarah, O BC ke bisector par bhi hai, isliye OB = OC hai. Isliye, OA = OB = OC hai.
O ek hi distance par hai

theorem.class10.three_point_circle.step4.text: Agar points collinear the, to bisectors parallel honge aur kabhi bhi milenge - isse collinear points ko circle par na rahne ki baat samjha jati hai!

theorem.class10.three_point_circle.title.text: Theorem 9.1: Three Non-Collinear Points Determine a Circle

theorem.class10.vector_diff.finalFormula: Vector AB, B ki position vector aur A ki position vector ki gunjaish ke beech ka antar hai.
theorem.class10.vector_diff.keyInsight: GPS navigation har second iska istemaal karta hai! Aapka phone aapki haliat kee gunjaish aur aapki gantavy kee gunjaish ko alag karta hai. Yeh physics aur engineering mein har gati ka pata lagane ka asas hai.

theorem.class10.vector_diff.practice_que.text: A=(1,2), B=(4,6). AB ka x-component kaise nikalna hai?

theorem.class10.vector_diff.step1_detail: Aap do delivery drones ko track kar rahe hain. Drone A (2, 3) par hai aur drone B (7, 5) par hai. Control tower (origin O) se unki position vectors ã aur b⃗ hain.
theorem.class10.vector_diff.step2_detail: O se B tak jaane ke liye A se: OA + AB = OB. Yeh vector addition ka triangle ka niyam hai.
theorem.class10.vector_diff.step3_detail: AB = OB − OA. Vector A se B tak, do position vectors ke beech ka antar hai.

theorem.class10.vector_diff.step4_detail.text: AB = (x₂−x₁, y₂−y₁). Aapko har direction mein displacement kaisa pata chalta hai. Magnitude |AB| = √((x₂−x₁)² + (y₂−y₁)²) aapko straight-line distance deta hai.

theorem.class10.vector_diff.title: Ek Vector ko Position Vectors se Paida Karna

theorem.class11.arithmetico_geometric_su.text: Yeh series arithmetic (ek constant d har term se jodta hai) aur geometric (har term ko r se ghana karta hai) ko milaati hai. Loan amortization schedules ke liye interest compound karte samay fixed payments ki gini hai!

theorem.class11.arithmetico_geometric_su: Pehle n terme likh kar unhe add karein

theorem.class11.arithmetico_geometric_su.text: a=1, d=2, r=3, n=2. S₂ (pehla term = a=1, doosra term = (a+d)r = 9) ko dekho.

Aap ek banker hain jo ek loan ka hisaab karte hain jo fixed payments aur compound interest ke saath hota hai. Pehla payment a hai, phir har baad ke payment d (inflation) se badhta hai, aur interest r ke rate se compound hota hai.
r se ghana karna: rSₙ = ar + (a+d)r² + (a+2d)r³ + ... + [a+(n−1)d]rⁿ.
Subtract karna: (1−r)Sₙ = a + dr + dr² + ... + drⁿ⁻¹ − (a+nd)rⁿ. A terms telescope karte hain, aur d terms ek geometric series banate hain!

Middle dr terms ek geometric series banate hain: dr(1+r+...+rⁿ⁻²) = dr(1−rⁿ⁻¹)/(1−r). (1−r) se divide karna complete formula deta hai. Isi tarah actuaries tiered annuity payments ka hisaab karte hain!

theorem.class11.binomial_theorem.finalFo.text: (a+b)ⁿ ko binomial coefficients ke terms ke sum ke roop mein expand karein.

theorem.class11.binomial_theorem.keyInsi.text: Binomial Theorem, probability (binomial distribution), finance (binomial options pricing model), aur fractal geometry (Pascal's Triangle Sierpinski triangle generate karta hai!) ke alag-alag kshetron mein ek algebraic powerhouse hai.

theorem.class11.binomial_theorem.practic: Use Pascal's Triangle row n, position k
Row 4 of Pascal's triangle: 1, 4, 6, 4, 1. Coefficient of a²b² = ⁴C₂ = 6
Find the coefficient of a²b² in (a+b)⁴.
theorem.class11.binomial_theorem.step1_d: 🌳 You're a geneticist tracking how a trait spreads through a family tree. Each generation inherits one allele from each parent. The expansion of (A+B)ⁿ gives all possible genotype combinations!
theorem.class11.binomial_theorem.step2_d: 📐 Assume the theorem holds for n = k: (a+b)ᵏ = Σ ᵏCᵢ aᵏ⁻ⁱ bⁱ.
theorem.class11.binomial_theorem.step3_d: 🧮 To prove for k+1, multiply both sides by (a+b): (a+b)ᵏ⁺¹ = (a+b)(ᵏC₀aᵏ + ᵏC₁aᵏ⁻¹b + ... + ᵏCₖbᵏ).
theorem.class11.binomial_theorem.step4_d: ✅ After distributing and grouping like terms, the coefficients follow Pascal's identity: ᵏCᵣ₋₁ + ᵏCᵣ = ᵏ⁺¹Cᵣ. Therefore (a+b)ᵏ⁺¹ = Σ ᵏ⁺¹Cᵣ aᵏ⁺¹⁻ʳ bʳ. By induction, the theorem holds for all positive integers n!

theorem.class11.combination_formula.fina.text: n distinct objects se r objects chunne ke tarah

theorem.class11.combination_formula.keyI.text: Chunav ke tarah, order ke bare mein koi fayda nahin hota. Agar aap 10 logon se ek 3-person committee chun rahe hain, to unhein kaise chun rahe hain, uska koi fayda nahin hota — ¹⁰C₃ = 120 tarah hain! Yah formula probability calculations ke liye asaani se kaam aati hai.

theorem.class11.combination_formula.prac.text: ⁵C₂ = 10. 5 logon se 2 chunne ke 10 tarah hain.

theorem.class11.combination_formula.step.text: Aap 5 volunteers se ek 3-person team organize kar rahe hain. Pehle aap permutations (ordered arrangements) ke bare mein sochte hain, lekin realize karte hain ki order ka koi fayda nahin hota — Alice, Bob, Charlie ko pick karne ka saman Charlie, Bob, Alice ko pick karne ka saman hai.
X = combinations ke number ko rakhein
📐 Har r objects ke combination ke liye, r! tarah ke arrangements ho sakte hain. Isliye, permutations (ordered) ke number = combinations ke number × arrangements: ⁿPᵣ = X × r!.
🧮 Hum jaante hain ⁿPᵣ = n!/(n−r)!. Substituting: X × r! = n!/(n−r)!.

✅ Dono taraf par r! se divide karein: X = ⁿCᵣ = n!/(r!(n−r)!). 5-choose-3 ke liye: ⁵C₃ = 5!/(3!2!) = 120/(6×2) = 10. 5 logon se 3-person teams banane ke 10 tarah hain. Yah binomial distribution aur Pascal's Triangle ka asar hai!

theorem.class11.complex_division_polar.f.text: Magnitude ko divide karein, angles ko subtract karein.

theorem.class11.complex_division_polar.k.text: Complex division polar form mein elegant hai: magnitude ko divide karein aur angles ko subtract karein! Yeh rectangular form se kafi aasan hai aur AC power systems ke liye phasor analysis mein use hota hai.

theorem.class11.complex_division_polar.p.text: z₁=4(cos 90°+i sin 90°), z₂=2(cos 30°+i sin 30°). Pata karein r₁/r₂.

theorem.class11.complex_division_polar.s.text: Ek electrical engineer ek power grid ko analyze kar raha hai. Do AC voltages hain: z₁ = r₁(cos θ₁ + i sin θ₁) aur z₂ = r₂(cos θ₂ + i sin θ₂). Unki qisam ko find karne ke liye, unhein polar form mein divide karein.
📐 Numerator aur denominator ko denominator ka conjugate se guna karein
🧮 Badhne ke baad: denominator cos²θ₂ + sin²θ₂ = 1 ban jata hai. Numerator angle sum/difference identities ka istemal karke saral hota hai.
Saral kar trig identities ka istemal karke
✅ cos θ₁ cos θ₂ + sin θ₁ sin θ₂ = cos(θ₁−θ₂) aur sin θ₁ cos θ₂ − cos θ₁ sin θ₂ = sin(θ₁−θ₂). Isliye z₁/z₂ = (r₁/r₂)[cos(θ₁−θ₂) + i sin(θ₁−θ₂)] — magnitude ko divide karein, angle ko subtract karein!
Aur difference identities ka upyog karein

theorem.class11.complex_division_polar.t.text: Complex Numbers ka Polar Form mein Division

theorem.class11.complex_inverse_polar.fi.text: Polar form mein multiplicative inverse ka use conjugate ka hota hai.

theorem.class11.complex_inverse_polar.ke.text: Real numbers ke liye 2⁻¹ = ½ jaisa, har non-zero complex number z = r(cos θ + i sin θ) ke liye inverse hota hai. Inverse ko magnitude r ko divide karke aur angle ko negate karke paya jaata hai!

theorem.class11.complex_inverse_polar.pr.text: |z⁻¹| = 1/2. Inverse ka magnitude reciprocal hota hai.

theorem.class11.complex_inverse_polar.st.text: 🔄 Aap ek electrical circuit design kar rahe hain jo AC signals ko complex numbers ke roop mein represent karta hai. Parallel component ka impedance nikalne ke liye, aapko Z ke 1/Z ko compute karna hoga. Shuruat mein z = r(cos θ + i sin θ) se, z⁻¹ = 1/z likhna hai.
📐 Rationalization karne ke liye, numerator aur denominator ko conjugate cos θ − i sin θ se multiply karein.
🧮 Kuchh is tarah hai: i² = −1: cos²θ − i² sin²θ = cos²θ + sin²θ = 1 (Pythagorean identity!). Denominator r × 1 = r mein badal jaata hai.
✅ z⁻¹ = (1/r)(cos θ − i sin θ). Magnitude 1/r aur angle −θ hai. AC circuits mein, yeh parallel circuit analysis ke liye essential hai!

theorem.class11.complex_inverse_polar.ti.text: Complex Number ka Inverse Polar Form mein

theorem.class11.complex_magnitude.finalF.text: z = x + iy ki magnitude uska complex plane mein mool sthan se doori hai.

theorem.class11.complex_magnitude.keyIns.text: Magnitude |z| ko modulus ya absolute value bhi kaha jata hai. Yeh complex number z = x + iy ki complex plane mein mool sthan se doori ko maapta hai, jaise ki distance formula! Yeh signal processing mein fundamental hai jahaan |z| ek signal ki amplitude ko darshata hai.

theorem.class11.complex_magnitude.practi.text: |3 + 4i| = 5. Complex plane par classic 3-4-5 triangle hai!

theorem.class11.complex_magnitude.step1_.text: Aap ek radar operator hain jo ek plane ko ek circular radar screen par track kar rahe hain. Plane ki position (x, y) par hai — real part x east-west coordinate hai, aur imaginary part y north-south coordinate hai. Centre (origin) se distance range ke liye zaroori hai.

theorem.class11.complex_magnitude.step2_.text: Magnitude ka definition |z| = √(x² + y²) hai. Kyonki real numbers ke squares hamesha ≥ 0 hota hai: x² ≥ 0 aur y² ≥ 0, isliye x² + y² ≥ 0. Ek non-negative number ka square root bhi non-negative hota hai, isliye |z| ≥ 0.

theorem.class11.complex_magnitude.step3_.text: Suppose |z| = 0. To √(x² + y²) = 0 → x² + y² = 0. Do squares ka sum sirf zero ho sakta hai agar dono zero ho: x = 0 aur y = 0. Isliye z = 0 + 0i = 0.

theorem.class11.complex_magnitude.step4_.text: Conversely, agar z = 0 = 0 + 0i, to |z| = √(0² + 0²) = 0. Isliye, |z| = 0 agar aur sirf agar z = 0. Yeh fact control systems mein feedback signal ki check karne ke liye use hota hai ki woh zero par converge ho gaya hai ya nahi!

theorem.class11.determinant_identical.fi.text: Do alag-alag rows ya columns wali matrix ka determinant zero hota hai.

theorem.class11.determinant_identical.ke.text: Yeh matlab hai: agar do rows ek hi hain, to matrix singular (non-invertible) hai. System of equations mein redundant information hota hai aur unique solution nahin hota. Isse lagta hai ki do hi directions se ek point ko pakadna hai!

theorem.class11.determinant_identical.pr.text: det(A) = 0. Identical rows se matrix singular hota hai.
Identical rows → determinant zero hona chahiye
Ek 3×3 matrix ke pehle aur doosre rows identical hain. det(A) kya hai?

theorem.class11.determinant_identical.st.text: Aapka GPS receiver teen satellite signals (rows) ka istemal karke aapki position ko triangulate karta hai. Agar do satellites identical signals (identical rows) bhejte hain, to receiver unhe distinguish nahin kar sakta hai — unique location nahin pata lag sakta hai!

Agar hum Row 1 aur Row 2 ko swap karte hain, kyunki ve identical hain, matrix B matrix A se identical hai. Lekin interchange property ke hisab se, row swap determinant sign ko flip karta hai: |B| = −|A|.
Kyun ki B = A (identical rows) hai, |B| = |A| hai. Lekin interchange property ke hisab se, |B| = −|A| bhi hai. Isliye |A| = −|A| hai.
Do expressions ko ek saath seta karein.
|A| ko dono sides par add karne se: 2|A| = 0 → |A| = 0. Isliye Gauss-Jordan elimination dependent rows ke liye break hota hai — determinant zero hone se matrix singular hota hai!

theorem.class11.determinant_interchange..text: Koi bhi do rows ya columns swap karne se determinant ko -1 se ghana hota hai.

Yeh property kehata hai ki determinant ek alternating multilinear function hai. Rows swap karne se ek parallelogram ki orientation flip ho jaati hai — signed area ka sign badal jaata hai! Computer graphics mein polygon winding order detection ke liye use hota hai.
Row swap karne par sign flip hota hai.

Rows swap karne se sign flip hota hai.
det(A) = 5 hai. Aap do rows swap karte hain. Naya determinant kya hai?

Aap ek system of 3 equations ko solve kar rahe hain, aur aapne pehle do equations ko galat se swap kar diya hai. Isse determinant kaise affect hota hai? Aap Row 1 aur Row 2 ko swap karke A se B banayein.
Do A ki do rows ko swap karke B banayein
Expanding |B| ki pehli row se ek alag-dalal expression milta hai.
B ko Row 1 se expand karein
Terms ko rearrange karke aur factor karke, bracket ke andar ki expression A ki pehli row se expansion se match hota hai.

Determinant ka sign badalta hai. Do rows swap karne se original sign wapas aata hai. Yeh property hai ki Cramer's rule ka kaam karta hai — aur yeh hai ki do identical rows wala determinant zero hona chahiye (kyonki unhe swap karke |A| = −|A| → 2|A| = 0 → |A| = 0 ho jata hai)!

theorem.class11.determinant_transpose.fi.text: Matrix ko transpose karne se uski determinant badal nahin jati.

theorem.class11.determinant_transpose.ke.text: Matrix ki determinant aur uski transpose ki determinant always barabar hoti hai. Isse row aur column operations ki prabhav identical hota hai — ek key symmetry jo linear algebra ki kai proof ko simplify karta hai!

theorem.class11.determinant_transpose.pr.text: A 2×2 matrix hai, det(A) = 7 hai. det(Aᵀ) kya hai?

theorem.class11.determinant_transpose.st.text: Aap ek cryptographer hain jo matrix encryption ke saath kaam karte hain. Matrix A = [[a₁₁, a₁₂, a₁₃], [a₂₁, a₂₂, a₂₃], [a₃₁, a₃₂, a₃₃]] aapki message ko encode karta hai. Transpose karne se determinant kaise badal jata hai?
Ek 3×3 matrix A ko consider karein.
📐 Aᵀ mein rows aur columns swap ho jate hain: Aᵀ = [[a₁₁, a₂₁, a₃₁], [a₁₂, a₂₂, a₃₂], [a₁₃, a₂₃, a₃₃]]. A ke position (i,j) mein element Aᵀ ke position (j,i) mein jaata hai.
🧮 Column 1 se |Aᵀ| expand karne se, Row 1 se |A| expand karne se ek hi expression milta hai. Har term match karta hai.
✅ Dono expansions identical hain, isliye |A| = |Aᵀ|. Isliye, determinants ke rows ke kisi bhi theorem automatically columns par bhi apply karta hai — ek powerful symmetry linear algebra mein!

theorem.class11.factor_theorem.finalForm.text: (x−c) p(x) ka ek factor hai agar aur hi p(c) = 0 hai.

theorem.class11.factor_theorem.keyInsigh.text: Yeh polynomials ko factor karne ke liye sabse powerful tool hai! Agar aap ek root guess kar sakte hain (jaise x=1, x=−1, x=2), to aap (x−c) ko factor kar sakte hain aur polynomial degree ko 1 reduce kar sakte hain. Yeh onion ki layers ko peel karne jaisa hai!

theorem.class11.factor_theorem.practice_.text: p(2) ko check karein. Agar p(2) = 0, toh yeh ek factor hai.

theorem.class11.factor_theorem.step1_det.text: Aap ek codebreaker hain jo ek jatil polynomial ko factor karne ki koshish kar rahe hain. Factor Theorem aapko ek niyamit tareeke se deta hai: agar aap ek value c jis par p(c) = 0 hota hai, toh aapne ek factor (x-c) pa liya hai!

theorem.class11.factor_theorem.step2_det.text: Agar (x-c) ek factor hai, to division p(x) ÷ (x-c) mein koi bhi baki nahin hota hai, isliye r = 0. Bain ul Theorem ke madhyam se, r = p(c). Isliye p(c) = 0.

theorem.class11.factor_theorem.step2_lab.text: Bain ul Theorem ke madhyam se

theorem.class11.factor_theorem.step3_det.text: Virodhi, agar p(c) = 0, to Bain ul Theorem ke madhyam se r = 0. r = 0 ko daalne par: p(x) = (x-c)q(x). Isse (x-c) ek factor hai iska proof hota hai.

theorem.class11.factor_theorem.step4_det.text: ✅ p(x) = x³ − 2x² − x + 2: p(1) = 1 − 2 − 1 + 2 = 0. Isliye (x-1) ek factor hai! Division karne par: p(x) = (x-1)(x² − x − 2) = (x-1)(x-2)(x+1). Ek root pa liya, baaki aasani se mil jata hai!

theorem.class11.fundamental_trig.finalFo: The cosine of a difference equals the product of cosines plus product of sines.
theorem.class11.fundamental_trig.keyInsi: This single formula is the mother of all trigonometric identities! From it, we can derive cos(α+β), sin(α±β), double-angle formulas, half-angle formulas, and product-to-sum identities. It's the Rosetta Stone of trigonometry.

theorem.class11.fundamental_trig.practic.text: cos(α−β) = cosαcosβ + sinαsinβ ka use karein
cos 30° = 0.8660. Formula puri tarah se kaam karti hai!
cos(60°−30°) = cos 60° cos 30° + sin 60° sin 30°. cos(30°) ka calculation karein.

theorem.class11.fundamental_trig.step1_d: 🎯 You're a GPS engineer. The unit circle represents the Earth's equator, and points C and D are two satellites at angles α and β from the prime meridian.

theorem.class11.fundamental_trig.step2_d.text: 📐 Yeh central angle C aur D ke beech hai α−β. Angle AOB = α−β ko construct karein A(1,0) aur B(cos(α−β), sin(α−β)) ke saath. Kyunki central angles barabar hain, chords CD aur AB barabar hain.

theorem.class11.fundamental_trig.step3_d.text: 🧮 CD² = AB² aur dono taraf ko expand karein. Bhai left: cos²α + cos²β − 2cosαcosβ + sin²α + sin²β − 2sinαsinβ = 2 − 2(cosαcosβ + sinαsinβ). Right: 1 − 2cos(α−β) + 1 = 2 − 2cos(α−β).

theorem.class11.fundamental_trig.step4_d.text: ✅ Barabar karein: 2 − 2(cosαcosβ + sinαsinβ) = 2 − 2cos(α−β). 2 ko cancel karein aur -2 se divide karein: cos(α−β) = cosαcosβ + sinαsinβ. Is single identity se sabhi trigonometry ke formulas nikalte hain!

theorem.class11.fundamental_trig.title: Fundamental Law of Trigonometry: cos(α−β)

theorem.class11.geometric_sum.finalFormu.text: Geometric series ka sum: Sₙ = a₁(1−rⁿ)/(1−r) jab r ≠ 1.

theorem.class11.geometric_sum.keyInsight.text: Yeh formula yeh batata hai ki ek ball jo har baar apni unchai ka 60% tak girta hai, ek sambhavit total distance tak jaata hai — infinite sum converge karta hai! Yeh bhi compound interest ke calculations ke liye kaam karta hai: aapka bank balance geometrically badhta hai.

theorem.class11.geometric_sum.practice_q.text: a₁ = 2, r = 3, n = 4. S₄ ko khojein.

theorem.class11.geometric_sum.step1_deta.text: 💰 Aap ek investor hain. Aap $1,000 ek fund mein daalne ke liye. Yeh fund har saal 5% badhta hai. Aapka balance n saal ke baad ek geometric series banata hai: saal 0: $1000, saal 1: $1050, saal 2: $1102.50... Har term pehle ka × 1.05 hai.

theorem.class11.geometric_sum.step2_deta.text: 📐 Entire series ko r se ghana karein: rSₙ = a₁r + a₁r² + a₁r³ + ... + a₁rⁿ. Yeh har term ko ek position pe shift karta hai.

theorem.class11.geometric_sum.step2_labe.text: Dono taraf r se ghana karna

theorem.class11.geometric_sum.step3_deta.text: 🧮 rSₙ se Sₙ ko kam karein: Sₙ − rSₙ = a₁ − a₁rⁿ. Sabhi intermediate terms (a₁r se a₁rⁿ⁻¹) puri tarah se cancel ho jaate hain — telescoping subtraction ki shaukat!

theorem.class11.geometric_sum.step4_deta.text: ✅ Sₙ(1−r) = a₁(1−rⁿ) → Sₙ = a₁(1−rⁿ)/(1−r). Infinite series ke liye (|r| < 1): S∞ = a₁/(1−r). Aapka $1000 5% par: agar aap isey aabadi se 5% kamane ke liye rakhte hain, to aapke sabhi bhavishya ke aavashyakon ka moolya = 1000/(1−0.05) = $20,000!

theorem.class11.geometric_sum.title.text: Geometric Series ke pehle n termon ka sum

theorem.class11.parallelogram_diagonals..text: Ek parallelogram ke dono diagonals ke midpoints ek dusre se milte hain.

Isliye crane ke parallelogram linkage ke liye load level rakhne ka reason yeh hai — diagonals hamesha midpoints par milte hain, jo ki arm angle ke hisab se bhi same relative position maintain karta hai!
M = (3.5, 4.5). Doosri diagonal BD ki usi centroid se milta hai.

Parallelogram with A(1,2), B(4,3), C(6,7). Find midpoint of diagonal AC.
🏗️ You're a structural engineer designing a parallelogram linkage for a construction crane. The four vertices of the parallelogram ABCD have position vectors ã, b⃗, c⃗, d⃗ from the origin.
📐 The midpoint M₁ of diagonal AC has position vector (ã + c⃗)/2 — the average of the two endpoint vectors.
📐 The midpoint M₂ of diagonal BD has position vector (b⃗ + d⃗)/2.
🧮 In a parallelogram, AB = DC. In vectors: b⃗ − ã = c⃗ − d⃗ → b⃗ + d⃗ = ã + c⃗ → (ã + c⃗)/2 = (b⃗ + d⃗)/2. The midpoints are identical — the diagonals bisect each other!

theorem.class11.parallelogram_diagonals..text: Ek Parallelogram ke diagonals ek dusre ko bisect karte hain

theorem.class11.remainder_theorem.finalF: X ko c se divide karne par baki baaki p(c) ke barabar hota hai
theorem.class11.remainder_theorem.keyIns: Yeh theorem aapko ek point par polynomial evaluate karne ka mauka deta hai, ismein synthetic division aur computer graphics mein fast polynomial evaluation (Horner's method) ke liye use hota hai.

theorem.class11.remainder_theorem.practi.text: p(x) = x² − 5x + 6. (x−2) se divide karne par raha kya hai?

theorem.class11.remainder_theorem.step1_: Aap ek data scientist hain, aap temperature data ko ek polynomial se fit kar rahe hain. Aapko ek specific time t = c par value janna hai. Remainder Theorem aapko isse compute karne ki zaroorat bina pata chala sakta hai!

theorem.class11.remainder_theorem.step2_.text: 📐 x = c ko substitute karein: p(c) = (c−c)q(c) + r. (c−c) term zero ho jata hai.

theorem.class11.remainder_theorem.step2_: X ko c se substitute karein

theorem.class11.remainder_theorem.step3_.text: 🧮 c−c = 0 hai, isliye (c−c)q(c) = 0 × q(c) = 0. Isliye p(c) = r. p(x) ko (x−c) se divide karne par raha p(c) hota hai — polynomial x = c par evaluate hota hai!

theorem.class11.remainder_theorem.step4_.text: ✅ Yeh theorem synthetic division ki basis hai — ek shortcut jo polynomials ko coefficients se hi divide karta hai. High-degree polynomials ke liye bahut taza hai aur computer graphics mein Bézier curve evaluation ke liye use hota hai (Horner's method O(n²) ko O(n) mein reduce karta hai)!

theorem.class11.remainder_theorem.title: Remainder Theorem

theorem.class11.trapezium_midpoint.final.text: Non-parallel sides ke midpoints ke beech mein join karne wala segment parallel sides ki sum ka adhikansh hai.

theorem.class11.trapezium_midpoint.keyIn.text: Pul designers is theorem ka istemal karte hain: trapezoidal bridge girder ki midline top aur bottom widths ki average hai. Iska istemal numerical integration (trapezoidal rule) mein bhi hota hai jahaan endpoints ki average integral ki approxamation karta hai!

theorem.class11.trapezium_midpoint.pract.text: AB=8cm aur DC=12cm (parallel) wala trapezium. Non-parallel sides ke midpoints ke joining segment ki length nikalna hai.

theorem.class11.trapezium_midpoint.step1.text: Aap ek civil engineer hain jo ek bridge banane ke liye trapezoidal cross-section ka design kar rahe hain. Top deck AB bottom base DC se parallel hai. Aapko mid-span support beam ki length nikalni hai.

theorem.class11.trapezium_midpoint.step2.text: M₁ AD ki side ka midpoint hai, M₂ BC ki side ka midpoint hai — yeh aapki mid-span beam ke endpoints hain.
M₁ aur M₂ ke midpoints hain

theorem.class11.trapezium_midpoint.step3.text: M₁M₂ = ½[(b⃗ − ã) + (c⃗ − d⃗)] = ½(AB + DC). Parallel side vectors ka sum, halke hue.

theorem.class11.trapezium_midpoint.step4.text: Kyonki AB ∥ DC, M₁M₂ bhi dono ke parallel hai. Uski length = ½(|AB| + |DC|). Top=10m aur bottom=16m wala bridge ke liye midspan = ½(10+16) = 13m. Isliye trapezoidal rule calculus mein endpoints ka average use karta hai!

theorem.class11.triangle_midpoint.finalF.text: Do sides ke centroids ke beech join karne wala segment third side ke parallel aur half hai.

theorem.class11.triangle_midpoint.keyIns.text: Yeh theorem intercept theorem ki foundation hai aur architecture mein truss structures banane ke liye use hota hai. Isse yeh bhi samajh aata hai ki triangle ki median usko do barabar kshetron mein divide karti hai!

theorem.class11.triangle_midpoint.practi.text: M₁M₂ = 3 = ½ × AB = ½ × 6. Sahee hai!
Triangle jismein A(0,0), B(6,0), C(2,4) hai. M₁M₂ (CA aur BC ke midpoints) ki length kya hai?

theorem.class11.triangle_midpoint.step1_.text: 🔺 Aap ek carpenter hain jo ek roof truss banate hain. Triangle ke vertices A(ã), B(b⃗), C(c⃗) hain. Aapko do sides ke centroids ke beech join karne wali beam khojni hai.
Triangle ke vertices ko define karein

theorem.class11.triangle_midpoint.step2_.text: 📐 M₁ yeh CA ke beech mein hai: (ã + c⃗)/2. M₂ yeh BC ke beech mein hai: (b⃗ + c⃗)/2. Yeh truss ke connection points hain.

theorem.class11.triangle_midpoint.step3_.text: 🧮 M₁M₂ = M₂ − M₁ = (b⃗ + c⃗)/2 − (ã + c⃗)/2 = ½(b⃗ − ã) = ½AB. c⃗ terms cancel ho jaate hain!

theorem.class11.triangle_midpoint.step4_.text: ✅ M₁M₂ = ½AB. Isse kuchh yeh hai: (1) M₁M₂ AB ke saath parallel hai (scalar multiple), aur (2) uski length AB ki length ki adhikansh hai. Aapka roof truss beam base ki adhikansh mein perfect position mein hai!

theorem.class11.trig_identities.finalFor.text: Double-angle formulas fundamental law se derive hote hain.

theorem.class11.trig_identities.keyInsig.text: Double aur triple angle formulas physics (harmonic oscillators, wave interference), engineering (Fourier series signals ko frequencies mein todne ke liye), aur music synthesis (base frequencies se overtones generate karne ke liye) mein essential hain.

theorem.class11.trig_identities.practice: sin(30°) = 0.5. Find sin(60°) using the double-angle formula.
theorem.class11.trig_identities.step1_de: 🧭 Navigation: If you turn 30° clockwise vs 30° counterclockwise, the eastward component is the same! By the fundamental law with α=0: cos(−β) = cos 0 cos β + sin 0 sin β = cos β.
theorem.class11.trig_identities.step2_de: 🔄 Using co-ratios: sin(α+β) = cos(π/2−(α+β)) = cos[(π/2−α)−β] = cos(π/2−α)cos β + sin(π/2−α)sin β = sin α cos β + cos α sin β.
theorem.class11.trig_identities.step3_de: 🧮 Let β = α in sin(α+β): sin(2α) = sin α cos α + cos α sin α = 2 sin α cos α. For cos: cos(2α) = cos²α − sin²α = 2cos²α − 1 = 1 − 2sin²α.
theorem.class11.trig_identities.step4_de: 🔊 Audio engineers use this: sin(3α) = sin(2α+α) = sin(2α)cos α + cos(2α)sin α = (2 sin α cos α)cos α + (1 − 2 sin²α)sin α = 2 sin α(1−sin²α) + sin α − 2 sin³α = 3 sin α − 4 sin³α. This generates the third harmonic in synthesizers!

theorem.class12.basic_integrals.finalFor.text: Basic integral formulas reverse differentiation se prapt hain.

theorem.class12.basic_integrals.keyInsig.text: Integration differentiation ka vipreet hai. Har differentiation rule integration rule deta hai! Power rule d/dx xⁿ⁺¹/(n+1) = xⁿ reverse karta hai ∫ xⁿ dx = xⁿ⁺¹/(n+1) + C. Ye sabhi integral calculus ka asas hai.

theorem.class12.basic_integrals.practice.text: x³ ka integral x⁴/4 hai. 0 se 2 tak: 2⁴/4 = 16/4 = 4
x³ ka integral kya hai? (x=2 se x=0 tak evaluate karein)

theorem.class12.basic_integrals.step1_de.text: Ek civil engineer irregular foundation ka volume calculate karta hai integration ka use karta hai. Agar cross-sectional area A(x) = x² hai, to x=0 se x=3 tak volume ∫₀³ x² dx = [x³/3]₀³ = 9 cubic meters hai.

theorem.class12.basic_integrals.step1_la.text: Power rule ko alag kar dein

theorem.class12.basic_integrals.step2_de.text: Power rule n = -1 par fail hota hai (zero se division ho jata hai). Lekin d/dx[ln x] = 1/x hai, isliye ∫ 1/x dx = ln|x| + C. Yeh algebraic aur logarithmic functions ke beech ka pahlu hai.

theorem.class12.basic_integrals.step3_de.text: aˣ/ln a ki derivative hai aˣ·ln a / ln a = aˣ. Isliye ∫ aˣ dx = aˣ/ln a + C. Natural exponential ke liye: ∫ eˣ dx = eˣ + C (kyonki ln e = 1).

theorem.class12.basic_integrals.step4_de.text: Integration linear hai: sum ka integral sum ka integral hai, aur constants nikal jaate hain. Isliye ∫ (3x² + 2x + 1) dx = 3·x³/3 + 2·x²/2 + x + C = x³ + x² + x + C.

theorem.class12.circle_general.finalForm: The general equation represents a circle with center (−g, −f) and radius √(g²+f²−c).
theorem.class12.circle_general.keyInsigh: By completing the square, any equation x² + y² + 2gx + 2fy + c = 0 can be rewritten as a circle. If g²+f²−c > 0, it’s a real circle; if equal to 0, it’s a point circle; if negative, the equation has no real graph. This is how GPS receivers determine your position from three circle equations!
theorem.class12.circle_general.step1_det: 🗺️ A GPS receiver detects signals from three satellites. Each satellite’s signal defines a circle: x² + y² + 2gx + 2fy + c = 0. The receiver needs to find the center and radius of each circle to triangulate your position.
theorem.class12.circle_general.step2_det: 📐 Separate the x and y terms, moving c to the right side.
theorem.class12.circle_general.step3_det: 🧮 Add g² (to complete (x+g)²) and f² (to complete (y+f)²). Balance by subtracting g² and f² on the same side.
theorem.class12.circle_general.step3_lab: Complete the square for x and y
theorem.class12.circle_general.step4_det: ✅ This matches the standard circle form (x−h)² + (y−k)² = r² where h = −g, k = −f, r = √(g²+f²−c). The center is (−g, −f) and radius is √(g²+f²−c). For a real circle, we need g²+f²−c > 0!
theorem.class12.circle_general.title: General Equation of a Circle
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.finalForm: Equation of the tangent to the circle at point P(x₁, y₁).
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.keyInsigh: The tangent to a circle at a point is perpendicular to the radius at that point. The formula x₁x + y₁y + g(x + x₁) + f(y + y₁) + c = 0 is derived by applying the slope condition. This is known as "T = 0" — replace x² → x₁x, y² → y₁y, x → (x+x₁)/2, y → (y+y₁)/2!
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.practice_: Circle x²+y²=25 at point (3,4). Tangent slope = −x₁/y₁ = −3/4 = ?
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.step1_det: 🎯 A machinist needs to cut a gear tooth that makes contact at a precise point on a circular gear. The cutting tool must follow the tangent line at the contact point.
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.step1_lab: Find the slope by implicit differentiation
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.step2_det: 📐 Differentiate implicitly: 2x + 2y·y' + 2g + 2f·y' = 0. Solve for y': dy/dx = −(x+g)/(y+f).
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.step3_det: 🧮 At point P(x₁, y₁), substitute into dy/dx: slope m = −(x₁+g)/(y₁+f). The tangent line in point-slope form: y − y₁ = m(x − x₁).
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.step4_det: ✅ Cross-multiply and rearrange. The result is the elegant T = 0 formula: replace x² → x₁x, y² → y₁y, 2gx → g(x+x₁), 2fy → f(y+y₁), c → c. The tangent equation is written without computing the derivative explicitly!
theorem.class12.circle_tangent.title: Tangent to a Circle at a Given Point

theorem.class12.concurrency_altitudes.fi.text: Triangle ke teen altitude aik hi jagah se milte hain (orthocenter).

theorem.class12.concurrency_altitudes.ke.text: Orthocenter, teen altitude ke intersection hai. Ek acute triangle mein yeh point triangle ke andar hai, aur ek obtuse triangle mein yeh point triangle ke bahar hai! Yeh concurrency hai jo teen altitude ke milne ka karan hai — ek adbhut geometric fact jo surveying mein position ko locate karne ke liye use hota hai.

theorem.class12.concurrency_altitudes.pr: Teen lines ek hi point par milne ka matlab kya hota hai?
Hint: Yeh 'con' se shuru hota hai aur lines of elevation se juda hai.
Triangle ke vertices (0,0), (4,0), (2,5) hain. Teen altitudes ek hi point par milte hain. Yeh property kaun hai?

theorem.class12.concurrency_altitudes.st.text: Ek surveyor ek triangular plot of land ko map kar raha hai. Orthocenter (jahan sab lines of elevation milte hain) ko find karne ke liye, ve teen altitude intersect karte hain ek hi point par prove karna hoga.
Vertices aur slopes ko define karein.
BC ki slope (y₃−y₂)/(x₃−x₂) hai. Altitude AD ∦ BC, isliye uski slope ki negative reciprocal hai: m_AD = −(x₃−x₂)/(y₃−y₂).
Point-slope form ka upyog karke point A(x₁, y₁) se: y − y₁ = m_AD(x − x₁). Rearranging: (x₃−x₂)x + (y₃−y₂)y − x₁(x₃−x₂) − y₁(y₃−y₂) = 0.

BE aur CF ke liye similar equations likhein. Unke coefficients ko ek determinant mein rakhein. R₂ + R₃ ko R₁ mein add karne se pehle row zero ho jati hai (kyunki har row se coefficients ka sum cancel hota hai). Zero row ka matlab determinant = 0 hai, jo lines concurrent hain orthocenter par prove karta hai!

theorem.class12.concurrency_altitudes.ti: Concurrency of Altitudes in a Triangle
theorem.class12.concurrency_bisectors.fi: Triangle ke side ke perpendicular bisectors ek hi point par milte hain, circumcenter!
theorem.class12.concurrency_bisectors.ke: Circumcenter woh point hai jo triangle ke sabhi vertices se ek hi doori par hota hai. Yeh point triangle ke sabhi vertices ko pass karne wali circle ka center hai. Yeh point teen perpendicular bisectors ke intersection par hota hai. Isliye, aap kisi bhi teen non-collinear points ke liye ek unique circle draw kar sakte hain!
theorem.class12.concurrency_bisectors.pr: What is the center of the circumscribed circle called?
theorem.class12.concurrency_bisectors.st: 📍 GPS triangulation: Three satellite positions A, B, C are known. To find your location, construct the perpendicular bisectors of AB and BC — their intersection is your position (circumcenter)!
BC ke midpoint aur slope kaun hai?
📐 The right bisector of BC passes through the midpoint with slope perpendicular to BC.
Right bisector ki equation kya hai?
🧮 Multiplying through and rearranging gives the standard linear equation. Note the constant terms involve squares of coordinates.
Simplify aur standard form mein likhein

✅ Write the equations for all three bisectors. The coefficient determinant has its first row become zero after adding R₂ + R₃. Zero determinant = concurrency. The intersection point (circumcenter) is equidistant from all three vertices — the center of the circumscribed circle!
theorem.class12.concurrency_medians.fina: The three medians of a triangle intersect at the centroid — the triangle’s center of mass.
theorem.class12.concurrency_medians.keyI: The centroid divides each median in a 2:1 ratio from the vertex to the midpoint. It’s the center of mass of the triangle — if you cut a triangle out of cardboard, it balances perfectly at the centroid! Engineers use this to find the center of gravity of triangular structures.
theorem.class12.concurrency_medians.prac: Triangle with vertices (0,0), (6,0), (3,6). Find the centroid x-coordinate.
theorem.class12.concurrency_medians.step: ⚖️ An architect is designing a triangular steel truss for a bridge roof. The center of mass (centroid) is where the medians intersect — this is where the main support column should be placed.
Median AD: vertex A to midpoint of BC
📐 The median AD passes through A(x₁, y₁) and the midpoint of BC. Using the two-point formula.
🧮 Cross-multiply and simplify to get a standard linear form.

✅ Write all three median equations. Their coefficient determinant becomes zero after adding rows, proving concurrency. The intersection point G = ((x₁+x₂+x₃)/3, (y₁+y₂+y₃)/3) — the centroid. This is also the average of the vertices, making it the center of mass!
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.finalFo: An ellipse with foci at (±c, 0) where c² = a²−b².
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.keyInsi: An ellipse is the set of points where the sum of distances to two foci is constant (2a). This is why planetary orbits are elliptical with the Sun at one focus (Kepler’s First Law). The flattening depends on eccentricity e = c/a = √(1−b²/a²)!
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.practic: Ellipse x²/25 + y²/9 = 1. Find c (distance from center to focus).
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.step1_d: 🌍 Johannes Kepler discovered that planets orbit the Sun in elliptical paths. The Sun is at one focus. The sum of distances from any point on the ellipse to the two foci is constant — the length of the major axis.
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.step1_l: Definition: sum of distances to foci = 2a
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.step2_d: 📐 Place foci at F₁(−c, 0) and F₂(c, 0). For any point P(x,y) on the ellipse: √[(x+c)²+y²] + √[(x−c)²+y²] = 2a.
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.step2_l: Set foci at (±c, 0) and point P(x, y)
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.step3_d: 🧮 Isolate one radical, square both sides, isolate the remaining radical, square again. After simplifying: (a²−c²)x² + a²y² = a²(a²−c²).
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.step4_d: ✅ Define b² = a²−c². Then b²x² + a²y² = a²b² → x²/a² + y²/b² = 1. For Earth’s orbit: a ≈ 149.6 million km, b ≈ 149.5 million km (nearly circular, e ≈ 0.017). Mars has e ≈ 0.093 — noticeably more elliptical!
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.step4_l: Let b² = a²−c² to get standard form
theorem.class12.ellipse_standard.title: Standard Equation of an Ellipse
theorem.class12.fundamental_theorem_calc: The definite integral equals the difference of the antiderivative at the bounds.

The FTC is the single most important theorem in calculus! It connects the two central concepts: differentiation and integration are inverse operations. This is why we can compute areas by finding antiderivatives — a profound link between geometry and algebra.
∫₁³ (2x) dx. Find F(3) − F(1) where F(x) = x².
🏔️ A geologist is calculating the volume of sediment in a river delta. The cross-sectional area varies with distance x from the shore. Define A(x) = ∫ₐˣ f(t) dt as the accumulated area from a to x.
📐 By definition: A'(x) = lim[A(x+h)−A(x)]/h as h→0. But A(x+h)−A(x) = ∫ₓˣ⁺ʰ f(t) dt ≈ f(x)·h by the Mean Value Theorem. So A'(x) = f(x). The area function is an antiderivative of f!
🧮 Since A'(x) = f(x), any antiderivative F of f differs from A by a constant: F(x) = A(x) + C. Then F(b)−F(a) = A(b)−A(a).

✅ But A(a) = ∫ₐᵃ f(t) dt = 0, so F(b)−F(a) = A(b) = ∫ₐᵇ f(x) dx. The definite integral equals the antiderivative evaluated at the bounds. The river delta volume: if A'(x) = 2x (linear increase), volume from x=0 to x=5 = [x²]₀⁵ = 25 cubic meters!
theorem.class12.homogeneous_second_degre: Every homogeneous second-degree equation represents a pair of lines through the origin.

A homogeneous equation ax² + 2hxy + by² = 0 has all terms of degree 2. It always factors into two linear equations, meaning it represents two straight lines through the origin. If h² − ab > 0, the lines are distinct and real — used in optics to analyze light cone sections!
Yes! x² − y² = (x−y)(x+y) = 0 is two lines through the origin: x = y and x = −y.
x² − y² = (x−y)(x+y) = 0 gives lines x=y and x=−y
Does x² − y² = 0 factor into two lines?
🔦 A physicist is analyzing a beam of light spreading from a point source. The light cone is described by a homogeneous equation: ax² + 2hxy + by² = 0. The goal is to factor it into two distinct rays.
📐 The key insight: complete a perfect square by adding and subtracting h²y².
🧮 The first three terms form (ax + hy)². The remaining terms become −y²(h²−ab).

✅ As a difference of squares: (ax + hy)² − (y√(h²−ab))² = 0. This factors into two linear equations: ax + y(h ± √(h²−ab)) = 0. Both pass through the origin (no constant term). If h² > ab: two distinct real lines. If h² = ab: one repeated line. If h² < ab: complex (imaginary) lines.
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.final: A hyperbola with foci at (±c, 0) where c² = a²+b².
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.keyIn: A hyperbola is the set of points where the difference of distances to two foci is constant (2a). Unlike the ellipse (sum is constant), the hyperbola has two separate branches. Used in navigation (LORAN systems) where the difference in signal arrival times from two stations locates a ship!
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.pract: Hyperbola x²/16 − y²/9 = 1. Find c (focus distance from center).
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.step1: 🚢 A ship uses LORAN navigation: two radio stations at F₁ and F₂ transmit synchronized signals. The ship measures the difference in arrival times. The locus of constant time difference is a hyperbola with foci at the stations.
Definition: difference of distances to foci = 2a
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.step2: 📐 The absolute difference of distances to F₁(−c,0) and F₂(c,0) equals 2a. Choose the branch where PF₁ > PF₂.
Set foci at (±c, 0) and point P(x, y)
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.step3: 🧮 Same double-squaring technique as the ellipse. But the sign difference (subtraction vs addition) leads to a different final form.
Square twice (same process as ellipse but sign differs)
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.step4: ✅ Define b² = c²−a². The standard form x²/a² − y²/b² = 1 has two branches (left and \). Asymptotes: y = ±(b/a)x. The LORAN ship: if stations are 100km apart (2c=100) and the constant difference is 60km (2a=60), then b² = 50²−30² = 1600, and the ship lies on x²/900 − y²/1600 = 1!
Let b² = c²−a² to get standard form
theorem.class12.hyperbola_standard.title: Standard Equation of a Hyperbola

theorem.class12.inverse_trig_derivatives.text: Inverse trig functions ki implicit differentiation se derivatives

Inverse trig derivatives integration problems mein aate hain (∫ 1/√(1−x²) dx = sin⁻¹ x + C). Ve physics mein bhi zaroori hain, jo angle ko ratios se nikalne ke liye calculate karte hain, jaise projectile ki launch angle ko velocity components se nikalne ke liye!

🎯 Ek ballistics expert vertical to horizontal velocity (x) ki ratio ko measure karta hai aur angle ki zaroorat hoti hai. Agar x = 0.5 hai, to y = sin⁻¹(0.5) = 30°. Implicit differentiation angle ko ratio ke saath kaise badalne ka pata karta hai.
y = sin⁻¹ x rakho ⇒ x = sin y
📐 Implicit differentiation ka istemal karke: d/dx(x) = d/dx(sin y) → 1 = cos y · dy/dx → dy/dx = 1/cos y.
🧮 cos²y + sin²y = 1 ka identity ka istemal karke: cos y = √(1−sin²y) = √(1−x²). Isliye d/dx sin⁻¹ x = 1/√(1−x²).

✅ tan⁻¹ ke liye: x = tan y ki differentiation karein → 1 = sec²y·dy/dx. Kyunki sec²y = 1 + tan²y = 1 + x², to hum paate hain d/dx tan⁻¹ x = 1/(1+x²). Ye formulas rational functions ki integration ke liye key hain!
tan⁻¹ x ki bhi same process

theorem.class12.parabola_standard.finalF: A parabola with focus at (a, 0) and directrix x = −a.
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.keyIns: A parabola is the set of points equidistant from a fixed point (focus) and a fixed line (directrix). This reflective property is why parabolic mirrors concentrate parallel light rays at the focus — used in telescopes, satellite dishes, and solar cookers!
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.practi: y² = 4ax. Solve for a from 4a = coefficient.
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.step1_: 📡 A satellite dish antenna has a parabolic shape. Signals arriving parallel to the axis reflect to the focus where the receiver is placed. The definition: every point P on the parabola is equidistant from the focus F and the directrix line.
Definition: distance to focus = distance to directrix
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.step2_: 📐 Choose coordinate axes so the focus F = (a, 0) and the directrix is x = −a. The distance from P(x,y) to F is √[(x−a)²+y²]. The distance to the directrix is |x−(−a)| = |x + a|.
Place focus at (a, 0) and directrix x = −a
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.step3_: 🧮 Square both sides (both are non-negative): (x−a)² + y² = (x+a)².
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.step4_: ✅ Expand: x²−2ax+a²+y² = x²+2ax+a². Cancel x² and a² on both sides. Solve: y² = 4ax. This is the standard equation of a parabola opening to the right. For satellite dishes: the receiver is at the focus (a, 0) and the dish follows y² = 4ax!
theorem.class12.parabola_standard.title: Standard Equation of a Parabola
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.finalFo: Condition for tangency: substitute y = mx + c into y² = 4ax and set discriminant = 0.
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.keyInsi: A line y = mx + c is tangent to y² = 4ax if c = a/m. This means the slope form of the tangent is y = mx + a/m. For a given slope m, there are exactly two parallel tangents (one on each side of the parabola), except for the vertical tangent at the vertex!
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.practic: Parabola y² = 8x. Line slope m = 2. Find c for tangency (c = a/m).
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.step1_d: 🎯 An architect is designing a parabolic arch and needs to find the tangent line at a point to calculate the support beam angle. Substitute the line equation y = mx + c into the parabola y² = 4ax.
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.step2_d: 📐 Expand: m²x² + 2mcx + c² = 4ax. Rearrange: m²x² + 2(mc−2a)x + c² = 0. This is a quadratic in x.
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.step3_d: 🧮 For tangency, the line must intersect at exactly one point. This means the quadratic has a double root: its discriminant D = b²−4ac must be zero.
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.step4_d: ✅ Expand: 4(m²c²−4amc+4a²) − 4m²c² = 0 → −16amc + 16a² = 0 → mc = a → c = a/m. The tangent equation in slope form: y = mx + a/m. This formula allows architects to find the support beam angle at any point on a parabolic arch!
theorem.class12.parabola_tangent.title: Tangent to a Parabola

theorem.class12.power_rule.finalFormulaD.text: xⁿ ki derivative n·xⁿ⁻¹ hai har positive integer n ke liye.

theorem.class12.power_rule.keyInsight.text: Power rule sab se zyada upyog kiya jana wala differentiation formula hai! Yeh polynomials ko simpler ones mein badal deta hai: har term ka exponent 1 girta hai aur naya coefficient ban jata hai. Physics (velocity = position ki derivative) se lekar economics (marginal cost se total cost) tak har jagah upyog kiya jata hai.

theorem.class12.power_rule.practice_hint.text: d/dx xⁿ = n·xⁿ⁻¹, isliye d/dx x⁵ = 5·x⁴

theorem.class12.power_rule.step1_detail.text: 📈 Ek rocket scientist ko spacecraft ki velocity har instant mein calculate karni hoti hai. Position s(t) = t³ hai. Derivative ki definition ke anusaar, velocity = derivative = h→0 tak ki limit (s(t+h)−s(t))/h hai.

theorem.class12.power_rule.step2_detail.text: 📐 (x+h)ⁿ ko binomial theorem ka istemal karke expand karein. Terms yeh hain: xⁿ + n·xⁿ⁻¹·h + n(n−1)/2!·xⁿ⁻²·h² + ... + hⁿ.

theorem.class12.power_rule.step2_label.text: Binomial theorem ka upyog karke expand karein

theorem.class12.power_rule.step3_detail.text: 🧮 xⁿ ko subtract karein aur h se divide karein. xⁿ cancel ho jata hai, aur har rahega h wala term. h ko factor karein: numerator h mein ho jata hai [n·xⁿ⁻¹ + ... + hⁿ⁻¹]. h ko denominator se cancel karein.

theorem.class12.power_rule.step3_label.text: xⁿ ki terms ko cancel karein

theorem.class12.power_rule.step4_detail.text: ✅ h→0 tak, sab terms jinke h hain, vanish ho jate hain, aur sirf n·xⁿ⁻¹ bachta hai. Rocket ke liye: agar s(t) = t³ hai, to velocity v(t) = 3t². t=5 seconds par: v = 3×25 = 75 m/s. Power rule differentiation ko instant bana deta hai!

theorem.class12.sin_derivative.finalForm.text: sin x ki derivative cos x hai.

theorem.class12.sin_derivative.keyInsigh.text: Yeh trigonometric calculus ki foundation hai! Sine ki derivative cosine hai kyonki sin x ki slope kisi bhi point par cos x ki value se barabar hoti hai. Yeh relationship simple harmonic motion (springs, pendulums) ko kyon aisa elegant bana deta hai: d²x/dt² = −ω²x.

theorem.class12.sin_derivative.step1_det.text: Ek audio engineer sound waves ko analyze karta hai aur amplitude ki badlaav ki jaankaari ke liye zaroori hai. Ek sine wave sin(440t) musical note A440 ko darshata hai. Badlaav ki rate speaker cone ki velocity deta hai.

theorem.class12.sin_derivative.step2_det.text: Identity sin(A+B) = sin A cos B + cos A sin B ka prayog karein: sin(x+h) = sin x cos h + cos x sin h.

theorem.class12.sin_derivative.step3_det.text: = lim[sin x(cos h − 1)/h + cos x(sin h)/h] ko factor karein. Do vishesh limits nikalte hain!

theorem.class12.sin_derivative.step4_det.text: Do moolbhoot limits: (1) lim(cos h − 1)/h = 0 (cos graph h=0 par flat hai), (2) lim(sin h)/h = 1 (chhoti angle ki takneek). Nataja: d/dx sin x = sin x·0 + cos x·1 = cos x. Isi tarah, d/dx cos x = −sin x.

theorem.class12.substitution_integrals.f.text: Standard integrals jo trigonometric substitution ke madhyam se prapt hote hain.

theorem.class12.substitution_integrals.k.text: Trigonometric substitution square roots ko trigonometric functions mein badal deti hai! √(a²−x²) → x = a sin θ → a cos θ, radical ko kam kar deti hai. Yeh technique Newton ne circular segment ka area nikalne ke liye upyog ki thi: ∫ √(a²−x²) dx circular segment ka area deta hai!

theorem.class12.substitution_integrals.p: Use the formula ∫ 1/√(a²−x²) dx = sin⁻¹(x/a) + C

theorem.class12.substitution_integrals.s.text: 📐 Ek astronomer circular telescope mirror ka area nikal raha hai. Radius a hai. Integral ∫ √(a²−x²) dx aata hai. x = a sin θ ko substitute karein, jo circle ka equation trigonometric form mein badal deti hai.
x = a sin θ ko substitute karein

theorem.class12.substitution_integrals.s: 🧮 √(a²−x²) = √(a²−a²sin²θ) = a√(1−sin²θ) = a cos θ. The integral becomes ∫ a cos θ · a cos θ dθ = a² ∫ cos²θ dθ.
📐 Use cos²θ = (1+cos 2θ)/2. Integrate: a²[θ/2 + sin 2θ/4] + C = (a²/2)θ + (a²/4) sin 2θ + C.

theorem.class12.substitution_integrals.s.text: half-angle ka upyog karke integrate karein

theorem.class12.substitution_integrals.s: ✅ Substituting back: ∫√(a²−x²)dx = (a²/2)sin⁻¹(x/a) + (x√(a²−x²))/2 + C. This formula gives the area of a circular segment of height x — used in civil engineering for water flow in partially filled pipes!

theorem.class9.angle_between_lines.final.text: Do intersecting lineon ke beech ki acute angle unke slopes se nikalne ke liye.

theorem.class9.angle_between_lines.keyIn.text: Road engineers use this to design safe intersection angles — a 90° crossing is safest because it maximizes visibility in all directions!

theorem.class9.angle_between_lines.pract.text: m₁ = 1, m₂ = 2. Unke beech acute angle ko khojna hai.

theorem.class9.angle_between_lines.step1.text: 🔄 Do roads ek intersection par milte hain. Ek mein ek gentle slope m₁ = 0.5 (ek gentle hill) hai, doosra steeper m₂ = 2 hai.

theorem.class9.angle_between_lines.step2.text: Yeh lines milne par, ve ek triangle banate hain x-axis ke saath. Yeh triangle ka exterior angle, do opposite interior angles ke sum ke barabar hota hai: α = β + θ.

theorem.class9.angle_between_lines.step3.text: θ = α − β. Tan ko dono taraf lagaayein: tan θ = tan(α − β). Tangent subtraction formula ka upyog karke: tan(α−β) = (tan α − tan β)/(1 + tan α × tan β).

theorem.class9.angle_between_lines.step4.text: Kyonki tan α = m₂ aur tan β = m₁: tan θ = (m₂ − m₁)/(1 + m₁m₂). Acute angle ke liye absolute value lene ke liye. Agar tan θ = 0, lines parallel hain. Agar 1 + m₁m₂ = 0, lines perpendicular hain!

theorem.class9.angle_between_lines.title.text: Do Lineon ke beech ki tooti hui angle

theorem.class9.change_base.finalFormulaD: Ek logarithm ko kisi bhi anya base mein badalna hai.
theorem.class9.change_base.keyInsight: Yeh THE most practical log kanoon hai kyunki yeh aapko calculator par log₁₀ ya ln button ka upyog karke kisi bhi log ko calculate karne ka mauka deta hai!
theorem.class9.change_base.practice_erro: log_b m = log_a m / log_a b
theorem.class9.change_base.practice_expl: log₂(100) ≈ 6.64. This formula lets you compute any log with common logs!
theorem.class9.change_base.practice_ques: log₁₀(100) = 2. Find log₂(100) using change of base.
theorem.class9.change_base.step1_detail: 📱 You're programming a calculator that only has log₁₀ (common log) and ln (natural log) buttons, but you need to compute log₂(8). This is the classic problem the Change of Base Law solves.
theorem.class9.change_base.step2_detail: Convert: m = b^x. We want to express this in terms of a known base a. Taking log_a of both sides: log_a m = log_a(b^x). Now we can use the Power Law in reverse!
theorem.class9.change_base.step3_detail: log_a m = x · log_a b. The exponent x "pulls down" in front. Now isolate x: x = log_a m / log_a b.
theorem.class9.change_base.step4_detail: Since x = log_b m, we get: log_b m = log_a m / log_a b. So log₂(8) = log₁₀(8) / log₁₀(2) = 0.9031 / 0.3010 = 3. Indeed, 2³ = 8.
theorem.class9.change_base.title: Base Badalne ki Kanoon

theorem.class9.distance_formula.finalFor.text: Kisi bhi do points ke beech ki doori nikalne ke liye coordinate plane par.

theorem.class9.distance_formula.keyInsig.text: Yeh formula GPS ki aadhar hai: aapka phone satelites aur aapki position ke beech ki doori yehi formula ka istemaal karke 3D mein 3D mein calculate karta hai.

theorem.class9.distance_formula.step1_de.text: Aap ek shahar planner hain. Point P₁(1, 2) library hai aur P₂(7, 6) hospital hai. Aapko unke beech ki straight-line doori nikalni hai taki aap ek naya road plan kar sakein.

theorem.class9.distance_formula.step2_de.text: P₁ aur P₂ se x-axis tak lines draw karein. Ye ek right triangle banate hain. Horizontal leg x₁ se x₂ tak chalti hai, aur vertical leg y₁ se y₂ tak chalti hai.

theorem.class9.distance_formula.step4_de.text: Yeh formula GPS ki aadhar hai: aapka phone satelites aur aapki position ke beech ki doori yehi formula ka istemaal karke 3D mein calculate karta hai.

theorem.class9.distributive_inter_union..translation: Intersection bhi union par distribute hota hai.

theorem.class9.distributive_inter_union..text: School library rule: you need a library card (A) AND (either a valid ID OR a teacher note). This is the same as (card AND ID) OR (card AND note).
theorem.class9.distributive_inter_union..translation: School library rule: aapko library card (A) aur (ya valid ID ya teacher note) ki zaroorat hai. Yeh (card aur ID) ya (card aur note) ke samaan hai.

theorem.class9.distributive_inter_union..text: A={1,2}, B={2,3}, C={3,4}. A∩(B∪C) mein kitne elements hain?
School library ka rule hai: aapko ek library card (A) aur (ya ek valid ID ya teacher note) (B ∪ C) ki zaroorat hai.

theorem.class9.distributive_inter_union..translation: Let x A ∩ (B ∪ C) mein ho

theorem.class9.distributive_inter_union..text: x A mein hai aur x (B ∪ C) mein hai. Isse yeh matlab hai x A mein hai aur (x B mein hai ya x C mein hai).
P aur (Q ya R) = (P aur Q) ya (P aur R). Isse: (x A mein hai aur x B mein hai) ya (x A mein hai aur x C mein hai).
x (A ∩ B) ya (A ∩ C) mein hai, isse yeh matlab hai x (A ∩ B) ∪ (A ∩ C) mein hai. Isse A ∩ (B ∪ C) (A ∩ B) ∪ (A ∩ C) ke barabar hai. Iski ulat direction bhi pramanik hai.
Sets ko phir se convert karein

theorem.class9.distributive_union_inter..text: Union distributive hai intersection par, jaise multiplication addition par.
Jaise 2×(3+4) = 2×3 + 2×4 arithmetic mein, set union distributive hai intersection par — yehi distributive kaunsa hai!

theorem.class9.distributive_union_inter..translation: A∪(B∩C) = {1,2,3}. (A∪B)∩(A∪C) se bhi samaan nateeja milta hai: {1,2,3}∩{1,2,4} = {1,2}
A={1,2}, B={2,3}, C={3,4}. A∪(B∩C) mein elements kya hain?

theorem.class9.distributive_union_inter..text: 🧩 Ek school prize ka soch lein: jeetne wale ek free period (A) ya extra recess aur homework pass (B ∩ C) dono milte hain.
x ∈ A ya x ∈ (B ∩ C). Isse kuchh hai, ya x ∈ A, ya (x ∈ B aur x ∈ C).
(P OR Q) AND (P OR R) logically equivalent hai P OR (Q AND R) se. Isliye: (x ∈ A OR x ∈ B) AND (x ∈ A OR x ∈ C).
(x ∈ A ∪ B) AND (x ∈ A ∪ C) means x ∈ (A ∪ B) ∩ (A ∪ C). So A ∪ (B ∩ C) ⊆ (A ∪ B) ∩ (A ∪ C). The reverse containment also holds. Just like 2×(3+4) = 2×3 + 2×4!
