theorem.class9.distributive_union_inter..translation: (x A ∪ B) aur (x A ∪ C) ka matlab hai x (A ∪ B) ∩ (A ∪ C) mein hai. Isliye A ∪ (B ∩ C) (A ∪ B) ∩ (A ∪ C) ke andar hai. Vah wapas bhi sahi hai. Jaisa ki 2×(3+4) = 2×3 + 2×4 hai!

theorem.class9.intersection_associative..text: Intersection ka kaam bhi associative hai.
Ek student Art Club, Music Club, aur Drama Club mein hai — sabhi teenon mein hona is par koi farq nahi hai ki aap clubs ko kaise group karte hain. Intersection sirf 'yeh sabhi sets mein hai.'
A={1,2,3}, B={2,3,4}, C={3,4,5}. (A∩B)∩C mein elements kya hain?
School mein teen clubs hain: Art Club A, Music Club B, aur Drama Club C. Teenon ki kshamata wale ek student sab teen kar sakta hai. Let y ∈ (A ∩ B) ∩ C.
y ∈ (A ∩ B) ∩ C ka matlab hai y ∈ (A ∩ B) aur y ∈ C. Aur y ∈ (A ∩ B) ka matlab hai y ∈ A aur y ∈ B. To: (y ∈ A aur y ∈ B) aur y ∈ C.
Logical aur hai associative: (P aur Q) aur R = P aur (Q aur R). To (y ∈ A aur y ∈ B) aur y ∈ C = y ∈ A aur (y ∈ B aur y ∈ C).
ANDs ko phir se group karein
y ∈ A aur (y ∈ B aur y ∈ C) ka matlab hai y ∈ A aur y ∈ (B ∩ C), jo ka matlab hai y ∈ A ∩ (B ∩ C). Virodhi shamilata pramanit karta hai barabar. Jaise union, intersection bhi associative hai!

theorem.class9.normal_form.finalFormulaD: Uttar se doori p aur ikleekta α ka istemaal karke equation banayein.
theorem.class9.normal_form.keyInsight: Yeh physics aur engineering mein point aur line ke beech ki doori nikalne aur optics mein wave fronts ki visheshagya ke liye bahut hi upyogi hai.

theorem.class9.normal_form.practice_erro.text: x cos α + y sin α = p

theorem.class9.normal_form.practice_ques.text: α = 60°, p = 5. cos α aur x cos α + y sin α ko x=2, y=3 par evaluate karein.

theorem.class9.normal_form.step1_detail.text: Ek line L aur uski perpendicular O se L tak ki line jo point C par milte hain. Is perpendicular ki line x-axis se ki tooti hui angle α hai. Distance OC = p hai.

theorem.class9.normal_form.step2_detail.text: Triangle OCA se: cos α = p/OA → OA = p/cos α (x-intercept). Triangle OCB se: sin α = p/OB → OB = p/sin α (y-intercept).

theorem.class9.normal_form.step3_detail.text: x/a + y/b = 1: x/(p/cos α) + y/(p/sin α) = 1 → (x cos α)/p + (y sin α)/p = 1.

theorem.class9.normal_form.step4_detail.text: x cos α + y sin α = p. Yeh Normal Form hai! Yeh physics aur engineering mein point aur line ke beech ki distance nikalne ke liye bahut hi upyogi hai.

theorem.class9.normal_form.title: Line ki Normal Form

theorem.class9.point_slope.finalFormulaD.text: Ek line ke liye point aur slope dono diye gaye hain. Uski equation kya hai?

theorem.class9.point_slope.keyInsight.text: Is calculus, physics, aur engineering mein aapko ek point aur badlaav ki dar (slope) ke baare mein pata hona bahut hi upyogi hota hai.

theorem.class9.point_slope.practice_expl.text: y = 10. Yeh y-y₁ = m(x-x₁) ka istemaal karke

theorem.class9.point_slope.practice_ques.text: Point (1,2) se guzarti line aur m = 4 hai. X = 3 hone par y kaunsi hogi?

theorem.class9.point_slope.step1_detail.text: 📍 Aapko pata hai ki ek line point (2, 3) se guzarti hai aur uski slope m = 1.5 hai. Aapko line ki equation pata karni hogi taaki aap isey apne digital map par graph kar saken.

theorem.class9.point_slope.step2_detail.text: P(x, y) ko line par koi bhi point kehna hai. B(x₁, y₁) = (2, 3) aur slope m = (y − y₁)/(x − x₁).

theorem.class9.point_slope.step3_detail.text: m(x − x₁) = y − y₁. Yeh equation kehta hai ki line par koi bhi point hota hai, to (x₁, y₁) se (x, y) tak ki slope har baar m hoti hai.

theorem.class9.point_slope.step4_detail.text: y − y₁ = m(x − x₁). Hamare example ke liye: y − 3 = 1.5(x − 2) → y = 1.5x.

theorem.class9.power_log.finalFormulaDes: Ek power ka log, base ka log ke gune ke barabar hota hai.
theorem.class9.power_log.keyInsight: Astronomers logon se pyar karte hain: sabse door galaxies tak ki doori 10²⁶ meters hai — log₁₀(10²⁶) = 26 × log₁₀(10) = 26. Ek 26 log scale par aisa likhna aur compare karna aasaan hai!
theorem.class9.power_log.practice_explan: log₂(81) = 4 × 1.585 = 6.34. Power log ke aage nikalta hai!
theorem.class9.power_log.practice_hint: 81 = 3⁴. Power law ka upyog karein: 4 × log₂(3)
theorem.class9.power_log.step1_detail: Aap ek investor hain jo apni paise ki growth dekhte hain. Aapka initial investment m = $3,000 hai, jo b = 2× per year ki growth rate se badhta hai. n = 4 saal ke baad, aapka paise 2^4 se ghana hota hai. Let log_b m = x, meaning b^x = m.
theorem.class9.power_log.step2_detail: m = b^x ko convert karein. Aapka initial $3,000 = 2^x ka matlab hai x = log₂(3000) ≈ 11.55 — aapka investment ka 'doubling power'.
theorem.class9.power_log.step3_detail: Dono taraf ko n-th power tak utha do: m^n = (b^x)^n. Power of a power rule ka upyog karein: m^n = b^(nx). Aapka investment 4 saal ke baad: 3000^4? Nahi — yeh ek mathematical power hai, financial nahin.
theorem.class9.power_log.step3_label: Dono taraf ko power n tak utha do
theorem.class9.power_log.step4_detail: Converting m^n = b^(nx) ko log form mein badalne ke liye: log_b(m^n) = nx. x ko log_b m ke liye badalne par, log_b(m^n) = n log_b m. Exponent 'log ke aage nikalta hai!'
theorem.class9.power_log.title: Power Logarithms ki Niyam
theorem.class9.product_log.finalFormulaD: Product ka log, logs ke sum ke barabar hota hai
theorem.class9.product_log.keyInsight: Richter scale isme yeh kanoon ka istemaal karta hai: magnitude 8 ka zameer 4 ki tarah hi nahin hai, balki 10,000 gunea hai! Kyunki scale logarithmic hai, har poora number 10 ka ek factor jodta hai.
theorem.class9.product_log.practice_hint: 32 = 4 × 8. Product ka niyam ka upyog karein: log₂(4) + log₂(8)
theorem.class9.product_log.practice_ques: Log₂(4) = 2, log₂(8) = 3. Log₂(32) ko khojna hai.
theorem.class9.product_log.step1_detail: Aap ek sound engineer hain jo ek live concert ko mix kar rahe hain. Microphone sound ko power m = 4 units par uthata hai, aur amplifier n = 8 units jodta hai. Total gain nikalne ke liye, aap logarithms ka istemaal karte hain. Let log_b m = x aur log_b n = y — yeh signal ke 'logarithmic power' ko darshata hain.
theorem.class9.product_log.step2_detail: Wapas badalne par: m = b^x aur n = b^y. Hamare concert mein, agar b = 2 (binary amplification) ho, to m = 2^x aur n = 2^y. Exponential form mein, log values ke peeche se chhup rahe actual multipliers ka avsar hota hai.
theorem.class9.product_log.step3_detail: Ab m × n ko ghana karein: mn = b^x · b^y. Jab hum powers ko same base ke saath ghana karte hain, to exponents ko ADD karte hain: mn = b^(x+y). Yeh key insight hai — real world mein ghana, log world mein addition ban jata hai.
theorem.class9.product_log.step4_detail: Mn ka logaritmic roop, mn ke logaritmic roop ke sum ke barabar hota hai. X aur y ko logaritmic roop mein rakhte hain, aur aapko milta hai: log_b(mn) = log_b m + log_b n. Total sound power (product) individual log powers ke sum ke barabar hota hai.
theorem.class9.product_log.title: Product Law of Logarithms
theorem.class9.pythagorean_identities.fi: Pythagorean Theorem ko trigonometric roop mein dhaara diya gaya hai.
theorem.class9.pythagorean_identities.ke: Is ek ek identity se, hum isse utha sakte hain: sec²θ − tan²θ = 1 aur cosec²θ − cot²θ = 1. Ye signal processing se lekar orbital mechanics tak sabhi cheezon mein upyog kiye jaate hain!

theorem.class9.pythagorean_identities.pr.text: sin²(30°) + cos²(30°) = 1. Hamesha 1, koi bhi angle ho!

theorem.class9.pythagorean_identities.st.text: 📐 Kisi bhi right triangle mein jo legs a, b aur hypotenuse c hai: a² + b² = c². Yeh geometry ki sabse prasiddh sambandh hai.
sin θ = a/c aur cos θ = b/c. Dono ko square karein: sin²θ = a²/c² aur cos²θ = b²/c².
sin²θ + cos²θ = a²/c² + b²/c² = (a² + b²)/c² = c²/c² = 1. Triangle ke legs aur hypotenuse puri tarah se cancel ho jaate hain!
sin²θ + cos²θ = 1 — yeh θ ka koi bhi value ho, yeh sabse aam sambandh hai! Isse hum is prakaar derive kar sakte hain: sec²θ − tan²θ = 1 aur cosec²θ − cot²θ = 1.

theorem.class9.pythagorean_identities.st: Is identity ka janm hota hai
theorem.class9.quotient_identities.final: Tangent hai sine ko cosine se divide karna; cotangent hai uska ulat.
theorem.class9.quotient_identities.keyIn: Ek unit circle par, tangent angle θ hai y-coordinate ko x-coordinate se divide karna — radius line ki slope!
theorem.class9.quotient_identities.pract: tan θ = sin θ / cos θ
theorem.class9.quotient_identities.step1: 🔺 Aap ek building ke base par khade hain, uske upar dekhte hain. Elevation ka angle θ hai. sin θ = opposite/hypotenuse = a/c. cos θ = adjacent/hypotenuse = b/c. tan θ = opposite/adjacent = a/b.
Ek sahi triangle ka dhyan rakhein
theorem.class9.quotient_identities.step2: Sine θ ko cosine θ se divide karna, (a/c) / (b/c) = (a/c) × (c/b) = a/b. Hypotenuse (c) cancel ho jata hai! Aur a/b exactly tan θ hai.
Sine ko cosines se divide karein
theorem.class9.quotient_identities.step3: Isi tarah, cosine θ ko sine θ se divide karna, b/a = cot θ. Ye Quotient Identities hain. Ye primary ratios (sin, cos) ko secondary ones (tan, cot) se judate hain.
Isi tarah cotangent ke liye bhi
theorem.class9.quotient_identities.title: Quotient Trigonometric Identities
theorem.class9.quotient_log.finalFormula: Ek quotient ka log, logs ki kami ke barabar hota hai.
theorem.class9.quotient_log.keyInsight: pH = −log[H⁺] ka iska upyog hai: ek solution jiska pH 3 hai, pH 5 se 100 ghana aur asidik hai, kyunki 10^(5−3) = 10² = 100 logarithmic scale par hota hai.

theorem.class9.quotient_log.practice_exp.text: log₁₀(10) = 1. Quotient law humein sahi 1 bhi deti hai!

theorem.class9.quotient_log.practice_hin.text: 10 = 100/10. Quotient law ka upyog karein: log₁₀(100) − log₁₀(10)

theorem.class9.quotient_log.practice_que.text: log₁₀(100) = 2, log₁₀(10) = 1. log₁₀(10) ko khojna hai.

theorem.class9.quotient_log.step1_detail.text: Aap ek chemist hain jo ek solution ki concentration ko pehle aur baad dilution ke baad maap raha hain. Initial concentration m = 32 M (molar) hai aur dilution ke baad yeh girta hai n = 4 M. Chalo log_b m = x aur log_b n = y likh dein.

theorem.class9.quotient_log.step2_detail.text: Exponential form mein badal dein: m = b^x aur n = b^y. Base b = 10 (common log) hai, to 32 = 10^x aur 4 = 10^y. Exponents x aur y yeh powers of 10 hain jo humein yeh concentrations dete hain.

theorem.class9.quotient_log.step3_detail.text: m ko n se divide karein: m/n = b^x / b^y. Jab hum powers ke saath hi base ke saath divide karte hain, to exponents ko subtract karte hain: m/n = b^(x−y). Division real world mein log world mein subtraction ban jaati hai.

theorem.class9.quotient_log.step4_detail.text: m/n = b^(x−y) ko log form mein badal dein: log_b(m/n) = x − y. Vapas daalne par: log_b(m/n) = log_b m − log_b n. Log terms mein dilution factor yeh hai ki individual log concentrations ke beech ki difference hai.

theorem.class9.quotient_log.step4_label.text: Logarithm ko phir se log form mein badal dein

theorem.class9.quotient_log.title: Logarithm ka quotient kanoon

theorem.class9.slope_intercept.finalForm.text: Ek straight line ki equation jo slope m aur y-intercept c ke saath hai.

theorem.class9.slope_intercept.keyInsigh.text: Business mein, yeh linear cost functions ko model karta hai: Total Cost = Variable Cost × Units + Fixed Cost. Slope unit ki cost hai!

theorem.class9.slope_intercept.practice_.text: y = 14. Y = mx + c ka upyog karke.
Ek line ki slope m = 3 aur intercept c = 2 hai. X = 4 ho to y kya hai?

theorem.class9.slope_intercept.step1_det.text: Aap apne bachat ko track kar rahe hain. Aap $100 (y-intercept c = 1 ek scaled graph par) se shuru karte hain aur har hafte $2 bachat karte hain (slope m = 2). Line aapke samay ke saath aapki kuli bachat ko dikhata hai.

theorem.class9.slope_intercept.step1_lab.text: Slope aur intercept ko identify karein

theorem.class9.slope_intercept.step2_det.text: P(x, y) koi bhi point ho jo line par hai. Line y-axis ko cross karne wala point A(0, c) hai — aapke week 0 par shuru ki bachat.

theorem.class9.slope_intercept.step3_det.text: Slope m = (y − c) / (x − 0) = (y − c) / x. Isse A se P tak ki rise over run line ki constant slope se barabar hoti hai.

theorem.class9.slope_intercept.step4_det.text: m = (y − c)/x → y − c = mx → y = mx + c. Yehi hai! Sabhi straight lines is tarah likhe ja sakte hain.

theorem.class9.two_intercept_form.finalF.text: Line ki equation aapke x-intercept a aur y-intercept b ke madhyam se.

theorem.class9.two_intercept_form.keyIns.text: Economists use this to graph budget constraints: if you spend $a on food and $b on housing, x/a + y/b = 1 shows all possible spending combinations!

theorem.class9.two_intercept_form.practi: y = 2. X/a + y/b = 1 ka istemaal karke.

theorem.class9.two_intercept_form.practi.text: x-intercept = 6, y-intercept = 3. x = 2 ke liye y ko khojein.

theorem.class9.two_intercept_form.step1_.text: 🎯 Ek line x-axis par x = 4 (x-intercept a = 4) aur y-axis par y = 3 (y-intercept b = 3) par seet hoti hai.

theorem.class9.two_intercept_form.step2_.text: Slope m = (b − 0)/(0 − a) = b/(−a) = −b/a = −3/4. Line 4 right ke liye 3 down jaati hai.

theorem.class9.two_intercept_form.step3_.text: Point (0, b) ka istemal karke: y − b = (−b/a)x → ay − ab = −bx → bx + ay = ab.

theorem.class9.two_intercept_form.step4_.text: bx)/(ab) + (ay)/(ab) = 1 → x/a + y/b = 1. Yeh elegant form direct intercepts dikhata hai.

theorem.class9.two_point_form.finalFormu.text: Do pointon ke beech se line ki samikaran

theorem.class9.two_point_form.keyInsight.text: Yeh formula surveyors use hain boundaries ko map karne ke liye - do measured points ke beech se line!

theorem.class9.two_point_form.practice_e.text: y = 15. Two-Point Form ka use karke.

theorem.class9.two_point_form.practice_q.text: Line through (0,0) aur (2,6). X = 5 ke liye y kaun hai?

theorem.class9.two_point_form.step1_deta.text: Aapke paas do points hain A(1, 2) aur B(4, 8). Sirf ek straight line dono ke through jaati hai — unke beech ki sabse kam distance.

theorem.class9.two_point_form.step1_labe.text: Do points ek line ko define karte hain

theorem.class9.two_point_form.step2_deta.text: Slope m = (y₂ − y₁)/(x₂ − x₁) = (8 − 2)/(4 − 1) = 6/3 = 2. Yeh line 2 units up jaati hai har 1 unit horizontally.

theorem.class9.two_point_form.step3_deta.text: Ab humne m = 2 mil gaya hai, Point-Slope Form ka use karein: y − y₁ = m(x − x₁) → y − 2 = 2(x − 1).

theorem.class9.two_point_form.step4_deta.text: Slope formula ko directly daalne se: y − y₁ = ((y₂−y₁)/(x₂−x₁))(x − x₁). Yeh Two-Point Form hai.

theorem.class9.union_associative.finalFo.text: Union ka operation associative hai — grouping ka koi fayda nahin.

theorem.class9.union_associative.keyInsi.text: School fair ki analoogy: teen groups (food stall, games, raffle). Total combined team koi bhi tarah se group karne par same rehta hai — union sirf 'yeh seton mein se kam se kam ek mein hai'.

theorem.class9.union_associative.practic.text: (A aur B ka milya) aur C ka milya = A aur (B aur C ka milya)
(A aur B ka milya) aur C ka milya = {1,2,3,4} = A aur (B aur C ka milya). Vahhi 4 elements hain!
A aur B ka milya = {1,2,3}. Phir aur C ka milya = {1,2,3,4}
A={1,2}, B={2,3}, C={3,4}. (A aur B ka milya) aur C ka elements kya hain?

theorem.class9.union_associative.step1_d.text: 🏟️ School fair mein teen groups ke students ko sochein: Group A food stall chalata hai, Group B games chalata hai, aur Group C raffle chalata hai. X ko koi bhi student ke roop mein rakhein. Agar x (A ∪ B) ∪ C mein hai, to...

theorem.class9.union_associative.step1_l.text: X ko ek element ke roop mein rakhein

theorem.class9.union_associative.step2_d.text: x ∈ (A ∪ B) ∪ C ka matlab hai x ∈ (A ∪ B) ya x ∈ C. Aur x ∈ (A ∪ B) ka matlab hai x ∈ A ya x ∈ B. Isliye: (x ∈ A OR x ∈ B) OR x ∈ C.

theorem.class9.union_associative.step2_l.text: Union ki definition ka prayog karein

theorem.class9.union_associative.step3_d.text: Logical OR associative hai — (P OR Q) OR R = P OR (Q OR R). Isliye (x ∈ A OR x ∈ B) OR x ∈ C = x ∈ A OR (x ∈ B OR x ∈ C). School fair koi bhi tarah se teams ko group karne par koi fayda nahin.

theorem.class9.union_associative.step3_l.text: ORs ko phir se group karein

theorem.class9.union_associative.step4_d.text: x A mein hai ya (x B mein hai ya x C mein hai) ka matlab hai x A mein hai ya x (B aur C ka milya) mein hai, jo ka matlab hai x A aur (B aur C ka milya) mein hai. Virodhi shamilat hai jis se barabar hai. Isliye (A aur B ka milya) aur C ka milya = A aur (B aur C ka milya). Grouping ka koi farq nahi hai!

theorem.class9.union_associative.step4_l.text: Sets mein phir se badal dein
